source: Shared taxis are a quick and comfortable means to travel to nearby places, such as Paro (Nu 150) and Punakha (Nu 200).
reference: Teksi bilong serim wantaim narapela pasindia em i gutpela rot bilong sindaun gut na i go i kam hariap long ol ples i stap klostu, kain olsem Paro (Nu 150) na Punakha (Nu 200).

source: The Oyapock River Bridge is a cable-stayed bridge. It spans the Oyapock River to link the cities of Oiapoque in Brazil and Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock in French Guiana.
reference: Oyapock River Bridge em i wanpelad 'cable-stayed bridge'. Em i stap long Oyapock River na konektim ol siti bilong Oiapoque insait long Brazil na Saint-Georges de l'Oyapock insait long French Guiana.

source: The two towers rise to a height of 83 meters, it's 378 meters long and it has two lanes of 3.50 m wide.
reference: Tupela taua i kirap long mak bilong 83 mita, longpela bilong tupela em 378 mita na tupela i gat tupela 'lane' we i op olsem 3.50 m.

source: The vertical clearance under the bridge is 15 meters. Construction was completed in August 2011, it didn't open to traffic until March 2017.
reference: Dispela 'vertical clearance' aninit long bris em i 15 mita. Konstraksen (construction) i bin pinis long Ogas 2011, em i no op long ol kar i ron i go inap long Mas 2017.

source: The bridge is scheduled to be fully operational in September 2017, when the Brazilian customs checkpoints are expected to be finished.
reference: Dispela bris em ol i makim taim bilong em long op long Septemba 2017, taim ol i pinisim ol 'customs checkpoints' bilong ol Brazil.

source: The Guaraní were the most significant indigenous group inhabiting what is now Eastern Paraguay, living as semi-nomadic hunters who also practised subsistence agriculture.
reference: Ol Gurani em ol impoten as ples manmeri we i stap nau long ples we ol i kolim Eastern Paraguay, na ol i save raun na pasinim abus na tu, ol i save planim kaikai long gaden.

source: The Chaco region was home to other groups of indigenous tribes such as the Guaycurú and Payaguá, who survived by hunting, gathering and fishing.
reference: Chaco rijen i gat ol narapela grup bilong ol asples manmeri kain olsem ol Guaycurú na Payaguá, husat i save painim abus, pis na bungim ol kaikai ol i painim long bus.

source: In the 16th century Paraguay, formerly called "The Giant Province of the Indies", was born as a result of the encounter of Spanish conquerors with the native indigenous groups.
reference: Long '16th century' Paraguay, we ol i bin kolim "The Giant Province of the Indies", i bin kamap taim ol lain Spain i kam bosim ol asples lain.

source: The Spaniards started the colonization period which lasted for three centuries.
reference: Ol lain long Spain i stat long bosim dispela hap long mak bilong tripela 'century'.

source: Since the foundation of Asunción in 1537, Paraguay has managed to keep a lot of its indigenous character and identity.
reference: Stat long taim ol i kamapim Asunción long 1537, Paraguay i bin larim planti bilong ol pasin na kalsa bilong ol asples grup i stap yet.

source: Argentina is well known for having one of the best polo teams and players in the world.
reference: Planti lain i save long Argentina olsem em i gat wanpela bilong ol nambawan 'polo team' na ol 'polo player' long olgeta hap bilong graun.

source: The largest tournament of the year takes place in December at the polo fields in Las Cañitas.
reference: Ol bikpela tonamen (tournament) bilong yia i save kamap long Disemba long ol 'polo fields' long Las Cañitas.

source: Smaller tournaments and matches can also be seen here at other times of the year.
reference: Ol liklik tonamen (tournament) na geim (game) em lain i ken lukim long hia tu long foapela taim long wanpela yia.

source: For news on tournaments and where to buy tickets for polo matches, check Asociacion Argentina de Polo.
reference: Long nius long ol tonamen (tournament) na wanem hap bilong baim tiket long ol polo geim (game), sekim Asociacion Argentina de Polo.

source: The official Falklands currency is the Falkland pound (FKP) whose value is set equivalent to that of one British pound (GBP).
reference: Karensi (currancy) bilong Falkland em Falkland pound (FKP) we velu bilong em i wankain olsem wanpela British pound (GBP).

source: Money can be exchanged at the only bank in the islands which is located in Stanley across from the FIC West store.
reference: Yu ken senisim mani long wanpela benk (bank) tasol insait long dispela ailan na em i stap long Stanley long narapela sait bilong FIC West store.

source: British pounds will generally be accepted anywhere in the islands and within Stanley credit cards and United States dollars are also often accepted.
reference: Yu ken yusim ol British pound long olgeta hap bilong ailan na insait long ol Stanley credit cards na tu ol yu ken yusim United States dollar.

source: On the outlying islands credit cards will probably not be accepted, although British and United States currency may be taken; check with the owners in advance to determine what is an acceptable payment method.
reference: Long ol ailan i stap autsait em ol i no inap long yusim ol 'credit cards', maski ol i ken kisim karensi (currency) bilong Britain na United States; sekim ol papa o mama bilongol stua long save wanem em ol rot bilong yusim long baim samting.

source: It is nearly impossible to exchange Falklands currency outside of the islands, so exchange money prior to leaving the islands.
reference: Em i klostu long hat olgeta long senisim karensi (currency) bilong Falkland autsait long ol ailan, olsem na yu mas senisim mani bipo long yu lusim dispela ailan.

source: Since Montevideo is south of the Equator, it is summer there when it's winter in the Northern Hemisphere and vice versa.
reference: Bikos Montevideo i stap saut long Equator, em i save sama (summer) long hap taim em i winta (winter) long Northern Hemisphere na bihain em i save senis gen.

source: Montevideo is in the subtropics; in the summer months, temperatures above +30°C are common.
reference: Montevideo em i stap insait long 'subtropic'; insait long ol mun bilong sama (summer), tempritsa (temperature) bilong em i save stap antap long +30°C.

source: The winter can be deceptively chilly: temperatures rarely go below freezing, but the wind and humidity combine to make it feel colder than what the thermometer says.
reference: Long taim bilong winta (winter) ples i ken kol nogut tru: ol tempritsa (temperature) i no save go daun aninit long mak we ais i save kamap, tasol i win na wara i stap long win i save bung wantaim long mekim ples i pilim kol moa long wanem samting 'thermometer' i tok.

source: There are no particular "rainy" and "dry" seasons: the amount of rain stays roughly the same throughout the year.
reference: I no gat wanpela "taim bilong ren" na "taim bilong san": mak bilong ren i save stap klostu wankain mak long olgeta yia.

source: Though many of the animals in the park are used to seeing humans, the wildlife is nonetheless wild and should not be fed or disturbed.
reference: Maski planti bilong ol animal insait long park i save long lukim ol manmeri, dispela em ol wel animal na ol lain i no ken givim kaikai long ol o distebim ol.

source: According to park authorities, stay at least 100 yards/meters away from bears and wolves and 25 yards/meters from all other wild animals!
reference: Ol atoriti bilong park i tok olsem, ol lain i mas i stap 100yard/meter o moa longwe long ol bea na ol wulf na 25 yard/meter longwe long ol narapela wel animal!

source: No matter how docile they may look, bison, elk, moose, bears, and nearly all large animals can attack.
reference: Maski ol i luk olsem ol i stap isi, ol 'bison', 'elk', 'moose', bea, na klostu olgeta bikpela animal i ken pait.

source: Each year, dozens of visitors are injured because they didn't keep a proper distance. These animals are large, wild, and potentially dangerous, so give them their space.
reference: Long olgeta yia, planti visita i save kisim bagarap long bodi bikos ol i no i stap longwe gut. Ol dispela animal i bikpela, wel, na i ken kamapim birua, olsem na givim ol spes bilong ol.

source: In addition, be aware that odors attract bears and other wildlife, so avoid carrying or cooking odorous foods and keep a clean camp.
reference: Na tu, lukaut long ol smel we i save pulim ol bea na ol narapela animal, olsem na no ken karim o kukim kaikai we i gat strongpela smel na mekim kem i stap klin.

source: Apia is the capital of Samoa. The town is on the island of Upolu and has a population of just under 40,000.
reference: Apia em i kapitol bilong Samoa. Dispela taun i stap long ailan bilong Upolu na i gat populese we i aninit long 40,000.

source: Apia was founded in the 1850s and has been the official capital of Samoa since 1959.
reference: Apia em ol i bin kamapim em long 1850s na em i bin nambawan kapitol bilong Samoa stat long 1959 i kam inap nau.

source: The harbor was the site of an infamous naval standoff in 1889 when seven ships from Germany, the US, and Britain refused to leave the harbor.
reference: Dispela 'habor' em i ples we bikpela pait i bin kamap long 1889 taim sevenpela sip bilong Germany, US, na Britain i les long lusim dispela 'habor'.

source: All the ships were sunk, except for one British cruiser. Nearly 200 American and German lives were lost.
reference: Olgeta sip em ol i bin sinlk, tasol wanpela 'British cruiser' i no sink. Klostu 200-pela laip bilong ol America na Germany i bin lus.

source: During the struggle for independence organised by the Mau movement, a peaceful gathering in the town resulted in the killing of the paramount chief Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III.
reference: Long pait bilong kisim indipendens (independence) we Mau muvmen (movement) i bin oganaisim, wanpela bung i bin kamap long taun na namel long dispela ''paramount chief' Tupua Tamasese Lealofi III i bin dai.

source: There are many beaches, due to Auckland's straddling of two harbours. The most popular ones are in three areas.
reference: I gat planti nambis, bikos Auckland i gat tupela basis i stap sait sait bilong em. Ol gutpela nambis we planti lain i save laikim i stap long tripela eria.

source: North Shore beaches (in North Harbour district) are on the Pacific Ocean and stretch from Long Bay in the north to Devonport in the south.
reference: Ol nambis we i stap long North Shores (long North Habour district) em ol i stap long Pacific Ocean na i stat long Long Bay long hap not na i go long Devenport long hap saut.

source: They are almost all sandy beaches with safe swimming, and most have shade provided by pohutukawa trees.
reference: Klostu olgeta bilong ol nambis em i seif long swim na planti bilong ol i gat gutpela seid (shade) bilong ol pohuukawa diwai long hait long san.

source: Tamaki Drive beaches are on the Waitemata Harbour, in the upmarket suburbs of Mission Bay and St Heliers in Central Auckland.
reference: Ol nambis bilong Tamaki Drive i stap long Waitemata Harbour, long ol 'upmarket' komuniti bilong Mission Bay na St Heliers long Central Auckland.

source: These are sometimes-crowded family beaches with a good range of shops lining the shore. Swimming is safe.
reference: Dispela ol gutpela nambis i save pulap wantaim ol famili na i save i gat ol gutpela stua i stap long klostu long dispela nambis. Em i seif long swim long ol dispela nambis.

source: The main local beer is 'Number One', it is not a complex beer, but pleasant and refreshing. The other local beer is called "Manta".
reference: Nambawan lokel bia em 'Number One', em i no wanpela narakain bia, tasol em i nais na i mekim man i pilim gut. Wanpela narapela lokel bia em i "Manta".

source: There are many French wines to be had, but the New Zealand and Australian wines might travel better.
reference: I gat planti French wain long dring, tasol ol wain bilong New Zealand na Australia em i isi moa long kisim ol.

source: The local tap water is perfectly safe to drink, but bottled water is easy to find if you are fearful.
reference: Ol lokel wara long tep (tap) em i seif long dringim, tasol em isi long painim botol wara sapos yu pret long wara long tep.

source: For Australians, the idea of 'flat white' coffee is foreign. A short black is 'espresso', cappuccino comes heaped high with cream (not froth), and tea is served without milk.
reference: 'Flat white' kofi em i narakain samting long ol lain Australia. Wanpela 'short black' em i wanpela ' expresso', 'cappuccino' we i kam wantaim krim (i no 'froth'), na ol i no save mekim ti we i gat milk insait long en.

source: The hot chocolate is up to Belgian standards. Fruit juices are pricey but excellent.
reference: Ol 'hotpela chocolate' em i stap long stendet bilong ol Belgium. Em i bikpela man i long ol frut jus, tasol ol i nambawan.

source: Many trips to the reef are made all year around, and injuries due to any of these causes on the reef are rare.
reference: Ol i save mekim planti raun i go long ol rip long olgeta taim insait long wanpela yia, na bagarap i kamap long bodi long ol dispela kain samting i save kamap wanwan taim tasol.

source: Still, take advice from authorities, obey all signs, and pay close attention to safety warnings.
reference: Tasol, kisim tok save bilong ol atoriti, bihainim olgeta sain, na ritim na bihainim gut ol tok lukaut.

source: Box jellyfish occur near beaches and near river estuaries from October to April north of 1770. They can occasionally be found outside these times.
reference: Ol 'box jellyfish' i save kamap olgeta taim arere long nambis na arere long ol maus wara long Oktoba i go long Epril long hap nat long 1770. Sampela taim ol i ken kamap autsait long ol dispela taim.

source: Sharks do exist, however they rarely attack humans. Most sharks are scared of humans and would swim away.
reference: Ol sak i stap, tasol i no save kaikai man. Planti bilong ol sak i save pret long manmeri na i save swim i go longwe.

source: Saltwater Crocodiles do not actively live in the ocean, their primary habitat is in river estuaries north from Rockhampton.
reference: Saltwater Crocodiles i no save stap long solwara olgeta taim, nambawan ples bilong ol long i stap em long ol maus wara we i stap long hap not long Rockhampton.

source: Booking in advance gives the traveller peace of mind that they will have somewhere to sleep once they arrive at their destination.
reference: Pasin bilong makim spes bipo long travela i go long dispela ples bai mekim tingting bilong ol i stap isi olsem ol bai gat sampela hap bilong slip taim ol i kamap long hap.

source: Travel agents often have deals with specific hotels, although you may find it possible to book other forms of accommodation, like camping grounds, through a travel agent.
reference: Planti taim ol 'travel agent' i save wok wantaim wanwan hotel, yu bai lukim olsem yu ken yusim wanpela lokel 'agent' long makim spes bilong stap long o narapela hap, kain olsem ol 'camping ground'.

source: Travel agents usually offer packages that include breakfast, transportation arrangements to/from the airport or even combined flight and hotel packages.
reference: Ol 'travel agent' i save givim ol offer' we i gat brekfas (breakfast), kar bilong kisim yu i go i kam long eapot o bungim tu ol prais bilong balus tiket na prais bilong hotel.

source: They can also hold the reservation for you if you need time to think about the offer or procure other documents for your destination (e.g. visa).
reference: Ol i ken mekim wanpela 'reservation' bilong yu sapos yu nidim taim long tingting long 'offer' o kisim ol narapela dokumen long go long dispela ples (eksampel, 'visa').

source: Any amendments or requests though should be coursed through the travel agent first and not directly with the hotel.
reference: Sampela senis o rikwes em yu mas tokim 'travel agent' pastaim na i no go stret long hotel.

source: For some festivals, the vast majority of the attendants to music festivals decide to camp on site, and most attendants consider it a vital part of the experience.
reference: Long ol sampela bikde, bikpela namba bilong ol lain i save pilai musik i mekim kem long dispela ples, na planti bilong ol i save pilim olsem dispela em i impoten hap bilong dispela ekspiriens.

source: If you want to be close to the action you're going to have to get in early to get a camping site close to the music.
reference: Sapos yu laik stap klostu long ol samting na lukim yu bai nid long go hariap na kisim wanpela ples bilong mekim kem klostu long musik.

source: Remember that even though music on the main stages may have finished, there may be sections of the festival that will keep playing music until late into the night.
reference: Tingim olsem maski musik long ol bikpela hap bilong bikde em i pinis, i gat sampela seksen bilong bikde we ol bai pilai musik i go inap long biknait.

source: Some festivals have special camping areas for families with young children.
reference: Sampela bikde i gat spesol eria bilong famili we i gat liklik pikinini long mekim kem bilong ol.

source: If crossing the Northern Baltic in winter, check the cabin location, as going through ice causes quite horrible noise for those most affected.
reference: Sapos ol i brukim Northern Baltic long winta (winter), sekim lokesen bilong 'cabin', bikos taim em i go namel long ais em i kamap wanpela bikpela nois long ol hap we i bamim ais.

source: Saint Petersburg cruises include time in town. Cruise passengers are exempted from visa requirements (check the terms).
reference: Ol 'cruise' i go long Saint Petersburg i save givim taim long raun long taun. Ol pasindia bilong 'cruise' i no nid long karim visa (lukim ol 'terms').

source: Casinos typically make many efforts to maximize time and money spent by guests. Windows and clocks are usually absent, and exits can be hard to find.
reference: Ol 'casino' i save traim hat tru long yusim taim na mani we ol 'guest' i kam na spendim. I no gat windua na klok, na ol dua bilong go autsait em i save hat long painim.

source: They usually have special food, drink and entertainment offers, to keep guests in a good mood, and keep them at the premise.
reference: Ol i save gat spesol kaikai, dring na ektiviti bilon amamas, long mekim ol 'guest' i amamas, na mekim ol i stap insait long banis.

source: Some venues offer alcoholic beverages on the house. However, drunkenness impairs judgement, and all good gamblers know the importance of staying sober.
reference: Sampela bilong ol ples we i save givim ol fri dring we i gat 'alcohol' insait long en. Tasol, taim man i spak em i no inap long skelim gut samting, na olgeta gutpela man bilong 'gamble' i save olsem em i impoten long tingting bilong ol i mas stap stret.

source: Anyone who's going to drive at high latitudes or over mountain passes should consider the possibility of snow, ice, or freezing temperatures.
reference: Wanpela husat bai draiv long ples we i stap antap moa o antap long ol maunten i mas tingim olsem i ken i gat sno (snow), ais, o tempritsa (temperature) we i kol nogut tru.

source: On icy and snowy roadways, friction is low and you cannot drive as if you were on bare asphalt.
reference: Long ol rot we i gat sno (snow) o ais, 'friction' em i no strong na yu no inap long draiv olsem yu save draiv antap long kolta nating.

source: During blizzards, enough snow to get you stuck can fall in very little time.
reference: Long taim bilong 'blizzard', inap sno (snow) we i ken mekim yu i pas i ken pundaun long sotpela taim tasol.

source: Visibility may also be restricted by falling or blowing snow or by condensation or ice on vehicle windows.
reference: Na tu, yu bai i no inap long lukluk gut taim sno (snow) i pundaun o blo o win i kamap wara o i kamap ais long ol windua bilong kar.

source: On the other hand, icy and snowy conditions are normal in many countries, and traffic goes on mostly uninterrupted all year round.
reference: Long narapela sait bilong dispela samting, ol kondisen bilong ais na sno (snow) em i nomol long planti kantri, na ol kar i save ron i go i kam gut long olgeta de bilong yia.

source: Safaris are perhaps the greatest tourism draw in Africa and the highlight for many visitors.
reference: Ol Safari em ating ol i nambawan samting we i save kisim ol turis i go long Africa na ol i bikpela samting long planti ol visita.

source: The term safari in popular use refers to overland travel to view the stunning African wildlife, particularly on savanna.
reference: Dispela hap tok 'safari' we planti lain i save yusim em i toktok long travel long graun long lukim ol narakain animal bilong Africa, planti bilong ol em ol animal bilong 'savanna'.

source: Some animals, such as elephants and giraffes, tend to approach closely to cars and standard equipment will allow good viewing.
reference: Sampela ol animal, kain olsem ol elefen na 'giraffe', i save laik long kam klostu long ol kar na ol stendet ikwipmen (equipment) bai mekim yu i ken lukim ol gut.

source: Lions, cheetahs and leopards are sometimes shy and you will see them better with binoculars.
reference: Ol laion, 'cheetah' na 'leopard' em sampela taim ol i save sem long kam ples klia na yu bai lukim ol gut taim yu yusim ol 'binoculars'.

source: A walking safari (also called a "bush walk", "hiking safari", or going "footing") consists of hiking, either for a few hours or several days.
reference: Wanpela 'safari' bilong wokabaut (ol i save kolim tu olsem wanpela "'bush walk'", "hiking safari", o 'going "'footing'") em i pasin bilong wokabaut, long sampela aua o long sampela de.

source: The Paralympics will take place from 24 August to 5 September 2021. Some events will be held in other locations throughout Japan.
reference: Paralympics bai i kamap long 24 Ogas i go long 5 Septemba 2021. Sampela ol samting bai kamap long ol narapela lokesen long ol narapela ples long Japan.

source: Tokyo will be the only Asian city to have hosted two summer Olympics, having hosted the games in 1964.
reference: Tokyu bai kamap olsem wanpela siti tasol long Asia long hostim tupela sama (summer) Olympics, bikos em i bin hostim ol geim (game) long 1964.

source: If you booked your flights and accommodation for 2020 before the postponement was announced, you may have a tricky situation.
reference: Sapos yu bin makim taim bilong flai na ples bilong stap long 2020 bipo long ol i tokaut olsem ol i senisim taim, yu bai i stap long wanpela hatpela taim.

source: Cancellation policies vary, but as of late March most coronavirus-based cancellation policies don't extend to July 2020, when the Olympics had been scheduled.
reference: Ol polisi bilong kenselim (cancel) ol samting i no wankain, tasol stat long pinis bilong Mas planti bilong ol polisi bilong kenselim ol samting we i kamap long koronavaires i no surik i go long Julai 2020, taim ol i bin makim Olympics long kamap.

source: It's expected that most event tickets will cost between ¥2,500 and ¥130,000, with typical tickets costing around ¥7,000.
reference: Ol i tok olsem planti bilong ol tiket bai i stap namel long ¥2,500 na ¥130,000, olsem na mak bilong wanpela nomol tiket bai i kost olsem ¥7,000.

source: Ironing damp clothes can help them dry. Many hotels have an iron and ironing board available for loan, even if one is not present in the room.
reference: Pasin bilong ainim (iron) ol wet klos i ken helpim ol long drai. Planti ol hotel i gat ain na bod bilong ain i stap bilong yusim, maski sapos wanpela ain i no i stap ples klia insait long rum.

source: If an iron isn't available, or if you don't fancy wearing ironed socks, then you can try using a hairdryer, if available.
reference: Sapos wanpela ain i no i stap, o sapos yu no tingting long werim soks we ol i ainim, orait yu ken traim yusim wanpela 'hairdryer', sapos em i stap.

source: Be careful not to allow fabric to become too hot (which can cause shrinkage, or in extreme cases, scorch).
reference: Yu mas lukaut long no ken larim laplap i kamap hot tumas (dispela i ken kamapim mekim klos i kamap liklik, o sapos em i hot tumas, klos bai i paia).

source: There are different ways of purifying water, some more effective against specific threats.
reference: I gat ol kainkain we bilong mekim wara i kamap 'pure', sampela i save mekim wok bilong daunim sampela kain samting we i ken kamapim hevi.

source: In some areas boiling water for a minute is enough, in others several minutes are needed.
reference: Long sampela eria boilim wara long wanpela minit em i inap, long ol narapela eria yu bai nid long boilim wara long sampela minit.

source: Filters vary in effectiveness, and should you have a concern, then you should consider buying your water in a sealed bottle from a reputable company.
reference: Ol 'filter' em strong bilong wanwan i narakain, na yu mas tingting, bihaim yu mas tingting long baim wara long wanplela botol long wanpela gutpela kampani.

source: Travellers may encounter animal pests that they are not familiar with in their home regions.
reference: Ol travela i ken bungim kainkain animal bilong bagarapim klos na kaikai we ol i no klia long en insait long ples we ol i kam long en.

source: Pests can spoil food, cause irritation, or in a worse case cause allergic reactions, spread venom, or transmit infections.
reference: Ol 'pest' i ken bagarapim kaikai, mekim skin i skrap, o nogut moa ol i ken kamapim ol 'allergic reaction', spredim 'venom', o mekim sik i kalap long wanpela man i go long narapela.

source: Infectious diseases themselves, or dangerous animals that can injure or kill people by force, do not usually qualify as pests.
reference: Ol sik we i save kalap hariap, o ol animal we i save bagarapim ol kilim ol manmeri wantaim fos, em ol i no kam aninit long dispela nem 'pest'.

source: Duty free shopping is the opportunity to buy goods exempted from taxes and excises at certain locations.
reference: 'Duty free shopping' em i sans long baim ol samting we yu no inap long baim takis na 'excises' long sampela lokesen.

source: Travellers bound for countries with heavy taxation can sometimes save a considerable amount of money, especially on products such as alcoholic beverages and tobacco.
reference: Ol travela we i go long kantri we i gat bikpela takis sampela taim ol i ken seivim (save) bikpela mani, ol i ken seivim long ol prodak taim olsem ol 'alcoholic beverages' na 'tobacco'.

source: The stretch between Point Marion and Fairmont presents the most challenging driving conditions on the Buffalo-Pittsburgh Highway, passing frequently through isolated backwoods terrain.
reference: Dispela spes namel long Point Marion na Fairmont em i ol nambawan hatpela kondisen bilong draiv long Buffalo-Pittsburgh Highway, planti taim em i go insait long ol ples we i no gat planti lain na i stap longwe.

source: If you're not used to driving on country roads, keep your wits about you: steep grades, narrow lanes, and sharp curves predominate.
reference: Sapos yu no save long draiv long ol rot autsait long siti, yu mas strongim yu yet: i gat ol ples we i sut i go daun o i kam antap, i gat ol liklik rot we i no gat spes long ron, na ol sap kona.

source: Posted speed limits are noticeably lower than in previous and subsequent sections — commonly 35-40 mph (56-64 km/h) — and strict obedience to them is even more important than otherwise.
reference: Ol tok save long mak bilong spit i stap daunbilo moa long ol narapela seksen — planti taim em i 35-40 mph (56-64 km/h) — na pasin bilong bihainim ol gut em i moa impoten long sakim tok.

source: Curiously, though, mobile phone service is much stronger here than along many other stretches of the route, e.g. the Pennsylvania Wilds.
reference: Mipela i no save bilong wanem na ol mobail fon sevis i strong moa long hia na i no strong long planti ol narapela hap bilong dispela rot, kain olsem Pennsylvania Wilds.

source: German pastries are quite good, and in Bavaria, are quite rich and varied, similar to those of their southern neighbor, Austria.
reference: Ol 'German pastry' em ol i gutpela tru, na long Bavaria, ol i gat gutpela teist na i gat kainkain, wankain olsem ol dispela bilong ol lain i stap saut, ol Austria.

source: Fruit pastries are common, with apples cooked into pastries year round, and cherries and plums making their appearances during the summer.
reference: I gat ol planti 'fruit pastry', na i gat ol epol we ol i wokim 'pastry' long en i save stap long olgeta taim bilong yia, na ol 'cherry pastry' na 'plum pastry' em ol i save mekim long taim bilong sama (summer).

source: Many German baked goods also feature almonds, hazelnuts, and other tree nuts. Popular cakes often pair particularly well with a cup of strong coffee.
reference: Planti bilong ol kaikai bilong ol German i save gat ol 'almond', 'hazelnut', na ol narapela 'nut'. Ol keik (cake) we planti lain i save long en em i save go gut wantaim wanpela strongpela kap kofi.

source: If you want some small though rich pastries, try what depending on region are called Berliner, Pfannkuchen or Krapfen.
reference: Sapos yu laikim sampela liklik 'pastry' we i teist nais, traim dispela ol i save kolim Berliner, Pfannkuchen o Krapfen long wanwan rijen.

source: A curry is a dish based on herbs and spices, together with either meat or vegetables.
reference: Wanpela 'curry' em i wanpela kaikai we i yusim ol 'herb' na 'spice', wantaim ol abus o kumu.

source: A curry can be either "dry" or "wet" depending on the amount of liquid.
reference: Wanpela 'curry' em i ken "drai" o "wet" bihainim mak bilong wara yu yusim.

source: In inland regions of Northern India and Pakistan, yogurt is commonly used in curries; in Southern India and some other coastal regions of the subcontinent, coconut milk is commonly used.
reference: Long ol rijen insait long Northern India na Pakistan, ol i save yusim 'yogurt' insait long ol 'curry'; long Southern India na sampela 'coastal' rijen bilong dispela ples, ol i save yusim milk bilong kokonas.

source: With 17,000 islands to choose from, Indonesian food is an umbrella term covering a vast variety of regional cuisines found across the nation.
reference: I gat 17,000 ailan long makim wanpela long en, kaikai bilong Indonesia em i wanpela bikpela nem we i karampaim ol kainkain kaikai we yu ken painim long olgeta hap bilong Indonesia.

source: But, if used without further qualifiers, the term tends to mean the food originally from the central and eastern parts of the main island Java.
reference: Tasol, sapos yu yusim dispela tok na nating, dispela tok em bai min kaikai i kam long 'central' na 'eastern' hap bilong Java ailan.

source: Now widely available throughout the archipelago, Javanese cuisine features an array of simply seasoned dishes, the predominant flavorings the Javanese favor being peanuts, chillies, sugar (especially Javanese coconut sugar) and various aromatic spices.
reference: Nau em i stap long olgeta hap bilong grup ailan, kaikai bilong Java em i gat ol 'seasoned dishes', na namba fleiva bilong kaikai bilong Java em pinat, 'chilie', suga (dispela em kokonas sugar bilong Java) na ol kainkain 'aromatic spices'.

source: Stirrups are supports for the rider's feet that hang down on either side of the saddle.
reference: Ol 'stirrup' em ol sapot bilong lek bilong 'rider' we i hangamap i kam daun long sait sait bilong 'saddle'.

source: They provide greater stability for the rider but can have safety concerns due to the potential for a rider's feet to get stuck in them.
reference: Ol i save givim moa 'stability' long 'rider', tasol ol i ken kamapim hevi long sait bilong sefti bikos nogut lek bilong 'rider' i pas insait long ol.

source: If a rider is thrown from a horse but has a foot caught in the stirrup, they could be dragged if the horse runs away. To minimize this risk, a number of safety precautions can be taken.
reference: Sapos wanpela 'rider' i pundaun long wanpela hos, tasol lek bilong em i pas long 'stirrup', hos i ken pulim ol taim em i ranawe. Long daunim mak bilong dispela hevi long kamap, ol 'rider' i mas mekim samting long abrusim dispela.

source: First, most riders wear riding boots with a heel and a smooth, quite narrow, sole.
reference: Namba wan, planti ol 'rider' i save werim ol 'riding boots' we i gat wanpela 'heel' na wanpela 'sole' we i 'smooth' na longpela.

source: Next, some saddles, particularly English saddles, have safety bars that allow a stirrup leather to fall off the saddle if pulled backwards by a falling rider.
reference: Narapela samting, sampela 'saddle', dispela em ol English 'saddle', ol i gat ol 'safety bar' we i save larim 'stirrup leather' i pundaun i go daun long 'saddle' sapos wanpela 'rider' i pundaun i go baksait.

source: Cochamó Valley - Chile's premier climbing destination, known as the Yosemite of South America, with a variety of granite big walls and crags.
reference: Cochamó Valley -nambawan hap bilong mekim 'climbing' long Chile, em ol kolim Yosemite of South America, em i gat ol kainkain bikpela 'granite wall' na 'crag'.

source: Summits include breath-taking views from peaks. Climbers from all parts of the world are continually establishing new routes amongst its endless potential of walls.
reference: Het bilong maunten i gat ol naispela samting bilong lukim. Ol lain bilong klaim long olgeta hap bilong graun i wok long kamapim ol nupela rot namel long ol planti wol.

source: Downhill snowsports, which include skiing and snowboarding, are popular sports involving sliding down snow-covered terrain with skis or a snowboard attached to your feet.
reference: Ol 'downhill snowsport', we i gat 'skiing' na 'snowboarding', em ol 'sport' we planti lain i save long en na yu bai slaid (slide) i go daun long wanpela hap wantaim 'skis' o 'snowboard' we ol i pasim long lek bilong yu.

source: Skiing is a major travelling activity with many enthusiasts, occasionally known as "ski bums," planning entire vacations around skiing at a particular location.
reference: 'Skiing' em i wanpela ektiviti bilong raun we ol lain i save laik long mekim, ol i kolim "'ski bums'," ol i save plenim holide bilong ol long mekim 'skiing' long wanpela lokesen.

source: The idea of skiing is very old — cave paintings depicting skiers date back as far as 5000 BC!
reference: Dispela tingting bilong 'skiing' em i bilong bipo yet – i gat ol peinting (painting) long hol bilong ston we i soim ol lain i ski long taim i go bek olsem 5000 BC!

source: Downhill skiing as a sport goes back to at least the 17th century, and in 1861 the first recreational ski club was opened by Norwegians in Australia.
reference: 'Downhill skiing' olsem wanpela 'sport' em i go bek long kain olsem '17th century' o moa, na long 1861 ol Norwegians i opim namba wan 'recreational ski club' long Australia.

source: Backpacking by ski: This activity is also called backcountry ski, ski touring or ski hiking.
reference: 'Backpacking by ski': Dispela ektiviti em ol i save kolim tu olsem 'backcountry ski', 'ski touring' o 'ski hiking'.

source: It is related to but usually not involving alpine style ski touring or mountaineering, the latter ones done in steep terrain and requiring much stiffer skis and boots.
reference: Em i wankain, tasol ol i no save bungim wantaim 'alpine styel ski touring' o 'mountaineering', dispela laspela wan em ol i save mekim long ples we i sap na i nidim 'skis' na 'boots' we i sap moa.

source: Think of the skiing route as of a similar hiking route.
reference: Tingim 'skiing route' olsem em i wankain long 'hiking route'.

source: In good conditions you will be able to cover somewhat greater distances than walking – but only very seldom you will get the speeds of cross country skiing without a heavy backpack in groomed tracks.
reference: Long gutpela kodisen yu bai inap long karamapim ol bikpela hap moa sapos yu ski na i no wokabaut – tasol wanwan taim yu bai kisim ol spit bilong 'cross country skiing' taim yu no karim wanpela hevipela bek long ol dispela hap bilong ski.

source: Europe is a continent that is relatively small but with many independent countries. Under normal circumstances, travelling through multiple countries would mean having to go through visa applications and passport control multiple times.
reference: Europe em i wanpela kontinen (continent) we i liklik, tasol em i gat planti indipenden kantri. Planti taim, long raun i go long planti kantri em bai min olsem yu bai go insait long planti 'visa application' na 'passport control' moa long wanpela taim.

source: The Schengen zone, however, works somewhat like one country in this respect.
reference: Schengen zone, em i narakain, sampela taim em i save stap olsem wanpela kantri bilong em yet.

source: As long as you stay in this zone, you can generally cross borders without going through passport control checkpoints again.
reference: Sapos yu stap insait long dispela 'zone', yu ken kalapim boda na yu bai i no inap go long 'passport control checkpoint' gen.

source: Similarly, by having a Schengen visa, you do not need to apply for visas to each of the Schengen member countries separately, hence saving time, money and paperwork.
reference: Wankain, taim yu gat wanpela Schengen visa, yu no nid long aplai long ol visa long wanwan bilong ol Schengen memba kantri, na dispela seivim taim, mani na pepawok.

source: There is no universal definition for which manufactured items are antiques. Some tax agencies define goods older than 100 years as antiques.
reference: I no gat wanpela bikpela tok we olgeta lain i yusim long makim wanem ol samting em ol i 'antique'. Sampela 'agency' bilong kisim takis i save tok olsem ol samting we i gat moa long 100 krismas em ol i 'antique'.

source: The definition has geographic variations, where the age limit might be shorter in places such as North America than in Europe.
reference: Dispela hap tok i save senis long wanwan ples, long sampela ples kain olsem North America em namba bilong krismas i no bikpela tumas tasol long Europe em bai i bikpela.

source: Handicraft products might be defined as antiques, though they are younger than similar mass-produced goods.
reference: Ol 'handicraft' em ol i kolim ol olsem ol 'antique', maski ol i no gat bikpela krismas wankain olsem ol narapela samting we fektori (factory) i mekim planti long wanpela taim.

source: Reindeer husbandry is an important livelihood among the Sámi and the culture surrounding the trade is important also for many with other professions.
reference: Pasin bilong lukautim 'reindeer' em i wanpela impoten wok namel long ol Sámi na kalsa bilong dispela wok em i impoten tu long ol narapela lain we i gat narapela wok insait long komuniti bilong ol.

source: Even traditionally, though, not all Sámi have been involved in big scale reindeer husbandry, but lived from fishing, hunting and similar, having reindeer mostly as draft animals.
reference: Bipo, i no olgeta Sámi i bin stap insait long wok bilong lukautim planti lain 'reindeer', tasol ol i stap long painim pis, painim abus na kain olsem, planti bilong ol i save gat 'reindeer' bilong karim kago.

source: Today many Sámi work in modern trades. Tourism is an important income in Sápmi, the Sámi area.
reference: Tude planti Sámi i mekim ol wok bilong tude. Wok bilong givim sevis long turis em i save wanpela impoten rot bilong kisim mani long ol Sápmi, long Sámi eria.

source: Though it is widely used, especially among non-Romani, the word "Gypsy" is often considered offensive because of its associations with negative stereotypes and inaccurate perceptions of Romani people.
reference: Maski planti lain i save yusim, bikpela moa namel long ol lain we i bilong Romani, dispela hap tok "Gypsy" em i no gutpela tok bikos ol i save yusim long toktok long ol negatif samting na ol rong tingting long Romani manmeri.

source: If the country you will be visiting becomes subject to a travel advisory, your travel health insurance or your trip cancellation insurance may be affected.
reference: Sapos kantri we yu bai go long en em ol lain i tok save long sampela samting we yu mas lukaut long en, sampela senis bai kamap long 'travel health insurance' o 'trip cancellation insurance' bilong yu.

source: You may also wish to consult the advice of governments other than your own, but their advice is designed for their citizens.
reference: Na tu, yu ken kisim tok save i kam long gavman na i no bilong yu yet, tasol tok save bilong ol em i bilong ol sitisen (citizen) bilong ol.

source: As one example, American citizens in the Middle East might face different situations from Europeans or Arabs.
reference: Wanpela eksampel, ol sitisen bilong America long Middle East em ol bai i stap long narapela we long ol lain long Europe na ol Arab.

source: Advisories are merely a brief summary of the political situation in one country.
reference: Ol 'advisory' em ol sotpela tok save long ol samting i kamap insait long politiks bilong wanpela kantri.

source: The views presented are often cursory, general and oversimplified compared to the more detailed information available elsewhere.
reference: Ol tingting i stap long dispela tok save em ol i raitim hariap, i no gat planti infomesen na ol i mekim ol samting i kamap olsem i no gat planti samting i stap insait long en sapos yu skelim wantaim ol infomesen i stap long narapela hap.

source: Severe weather is the generic term for any dangerous weather phenomenon with the potential to cause damage, serious social disruption, or loss of human life.
reference: Weda (weather) nogut em i wanpela tok we i no tok klia tumas long ol weda we i ken kamapim bagarap, distebim laip bilong ol manmeri, mekim man i dai.

source: Severe weather can occur anywhere in the world, and there are different types of it, which can depend on geography, topography, and atmospheric conditions.
reference: Ol weda (weather) nogut i ken kamap long kainkain hap bilong dispela graun, na i gat kainkain, dispela em bai bihainim ples, envairomen, na ol kondisen bilong 'atmosphere'.

source: High winds, hail, excessive precipitation, and wildfires are forms and effects of severe weather, as are thunderstorms, tornadoes, waterspouts, and cyclones.
reference: Ol 'high winds', 'excessive precipitation', na 'wildfire' em ol sampela samtinng i ken kamap bikos long weda (weather) nogut, wankain tu em ol 'thunderstorm', 'tornado', 'waterspout', na 'cyclone'.

source: Regional and seasonal severe weather phenomena include blizzards, snowstorms, ice storms, and dust storms.
reference: Ol weda (weather) nogut bilong wanwan rijen o sisen em ol 'blizzard', 'snowstorm', 'ice storm', na 'dust storm'.

source: Travellers are strongly advised to be aware of any risk of severe weather affecting their area as they may affect any travel plans.
reference: I gat strongpela tok save i go long ol travela long save long ol weda (weather) we i save kamap long ol eria bikos ol bai mekim senis i kamap long ol plen bilong ol travela.

source: Anyone planning a visit to a country that could be considered a war zone should get professional training.
reference: Husat lain i mekim plen long go long wanpela kantri we woa i save kamap lng en i mas kisim treining (training) i kam long ol profesenol.

source: A search of the Internet for 'Hostile environment course' will probably provide the address of a local company.
reference: Wanpela 'search' long Internet long 'Hostile environment course' bai inap long givim adres bilong wanpela lokel kampani.

source: A course will normally cover all the issues discussed here in far greater detail, usually with practical experience.
reference: Wanpela kos bai karampaim ol samting we mipela i toktok long hia na bai givim moa infomesen, wantaim sampela ekspiriens.

source: A course will normally be from 2-5 days and will involve role play, a lot of first aid and sometimes weapons training.
reference: Wanpela kos bai stap long 2-5-pela de na bai i gat 'role play', planti 'first aid' na sampela treining bilong ol samting bilong pait.

source: Books and magazines dealing with wilderness survival are common, but publications dealing with war zones are few.
reference: Ol buk na megasin we i toktok long pasin bilong stap long ples nating i save stap long planti hap, tasol i no gat planti pablikesen we i save toktok long ol 'war zone'.

source: Voyagers planning sex reassignment surgery abroad must ensure they're carrying valid documents for the return trip.
reference: Ol lain bilong travel we i plen long go mekim 'sex reassignment surgery' long narapela kantri i mas ol 'valid documents' bilong ol long yusim taim ol i kam bek.

source: The willingness of governments to issue passports with gender not stated (X) or documents updated to match a desired name and gender varies.
reference: Pasin bilong wanwan gavman long givim ol paspot (passport) we ol i no makim (X) long man o meri o givim ol dokumen long soim senis long nem na man i go long meri o meri i go long man em i narakain narakain long wanwan gavman.

source: Willingness of foreign governments to honour these documents is just as widely variable.
reference: Pasin bilong gavman bilong narapela kantri long luksave long ol dispela dokumen em i narakain narakain tu.

source: Searches at security checkpoints have also become far more intrusive in the post-September 11, 2001 era.
reference: Pasin bilong sekuriti long sekim ol manmeri long ol 'checkpoint' i wok long kamap nogut moa bihain long Septemba 11, 2001.

source: Pre-operative transgender people should not expect to pass through the scanners with their privacy and dignity intact.
reference: Ol lain i laik senisim 'gender' bilong ol i no ken ting olsem ol bai go aninit long 'scanner' na 'privacy' bilong ol bai i stap hait.

source: Rip currents are the returning flow from waves breaking off the beach, often at a reef or similar.
reference: Ol 'rip currents' em ol tait bilong solwara we i bruk long nambis na i wok long kam bek, planti taim long wanpela rip o kain samting olsem.

source: Due to the underwater topology the return flow is concentrated at a few deeper sections, and a fast current to deep water may form there.
reference: Bikos long 'underwater typology' wara we i kam bek em i bung long ol ples we i dip, na wanpela 'fast current' i go long dispela hap we i dip i ken kamap long hap.

source: Most deaths happen as result of fatigue trying to swim back against the current, which may be impossible.
reference: Planti dai i save kamap bikos ol manmeri i pilim tait long traim long swim i kam bek agensim 'current', na dispela em i hat long mekim.

source: As soon as you get out of the current, swimming back is no more difficult than normally.
reference: Taim yu kam autsait long 'current', pasin bilong swim i go bek em bai i no inap hat long mekim.

source: Try aiming somewhere where you are not caught again or, depending on your skills and on whether you have been noticed, you might want to wait for rescue.
reference: Traim long swim i go long wanpela hap we 'current' i no pulim bek gen o, bihainim mak bilong yu long swim na saposl i gat lain i lukim yu, yu ken tingting long wet long ol lain i ken seivim (save) yu.

source: Re-entry shock comes on sooner than culture shock (there's less of a honeymoon phase), lasts longer, and can be more severe.
reference: 'Re-entry shock' i save kamap hariap moa long 'culture shock' (i no gat 'honeymoon phase'), na em i save stap longpela taim na em i ken kamap long mak we i bikpela moa long 'culture shock'.

source: Travellers who had an easy time adjusting to the new culture sometimes have a particularly hard time readjusting to their native culture.
reference: Ol travela husat i no save painim hat long lainim long stap long nupela kalsa i save painim hat long lainim gen hau long i stap long kalsa bilong ol yet.

source: When returning home after living abroad, you've adapted to the new culture and lost some of your habits from your home culture.
reference: Taim yu stap long narapela kantri na kam bek long haus bilong yu, yu senisim pasin bilong yu long bihainim nupela kalsa na yu lusim sampela bilong ol pasin bilong kalsa bilong yu yet.

source: When you went abroad at first, people were probably patient and understanding, knowing that travellers in a new country need to adapt.
reference: Taim yu bin i go long narapela kantri long namba wan taim tru, yu bin isi na traim long lainim samting, yu save olsem ol travela i stap long narapela kantri i mas senisim tingting na pasin bilong ol.

source: People may not anticipate that patience and understanding are also necessary for travellers returning home.
reference: Planti manmeri i no ting olsem pasin bilong i stap isi na lainim samting em impoten long ol travela (traveller) taim ol i kam bek gen long haus bilong ol.

source: The pyramid sound and light show is one of the most interesting things in the area for kids.
reference: 'Pyramid sound and light show' em i wanpela samting we ol pikinini i save laikim na em i stap long dispela eria.

source: You can see the pyramids in the dark and you can see them in silence before the show begins.
reference: Yu ken lukim ol 'pyramid' long tudak na yu ken lukim ol tu bipo long 'show' i stat.

source: Usually you always here the sound of tourists and vendors. The story of the sound and light is just like a story book.
reference: Olgeta taim yu bai harim saun bilong ol turis na ol lain bilong salim samting. Dispela stori bilong saun na lait em i wankain tru olsem wanpela stori buk.

source: The Sphinx is set as the backdrop and the narrator of a long story.
reference: 'Spinx' em i stap long baksait na em i stap olsem man i wok long autim stori.

source: The scenes are displayed on the pyramids and the different pyramids are lit up.
reference: Ol wanwan hap bilong stori em ol i soim antap long o 'pyramid' na ol i save mekim lait i kamap long ol kainkain 'pyramid'.

source: South Shetland Islands, discovered in 1819, are claimed by several nations and have the most bases, with sixteen active in 2020.
reference: South Shetland Islands, em ol i bin painim em long 1819, na moa long wanpela kantri i bin kisim em na em i gat planti senta, sikstinpela i bin ektif (active) yet long yia 2020.

source: The archipelago lies 120 km north of the Peninsula. The largest is King George Island with the settlement of Villa Las Estrellas.
reference: Dispela grup ailan i stap 120 km not long Peninsula. Nambawan bikpela ailan em King George Island we i gat ol man i stap long Villa Las Estrellas.

source: Others include Livingston Island, and Deception where the flooded caldera of a still-active volcano provides a spectacular natural harbour.
reference: Ol narapela em Livingston Island, na Deception we 'flooded caldera' bilong volkeno we i ektif (active) yet i save givim wanpela kain lukluk bilong dispela basis.

source: Ellsworth Land is the region south of the Peninsula, bounded by the Bellingshausen Sea.
reference: Ellsworth Land em i rijen we i stap long saut bilong Peninsula, na Bellingshausen Sea i raunim em.

source: The mountains of the Peninsula here merge into the plateau, then re-emerge to form the 360 km chain of the Ellsworth Mountains, bisected by the Minnesota Glacier.
reference: Ol maunten bilong Peninsula long hia i bungim wantaim ol graun i stap antap, na bihain em i stap ples klia gen na i kamapim 360 km sen (chain) bilong Ellsworth Mountains we Minnesota Glacier i brukim i go long tupela hap.

source: The northern part or Sentinel Range has Antarctica's highest mountains, the Vinson Massif, peaking at 4892 m Mount Vinson.
reference: Hap not bilong Sentinel Range i gat ol longpela maunten tru bilong Antarctica, Vinson Massif, na Mount Vinson we longpela bilong em i 4892 m.

source: In remote locations, without cell phone coverage, a satellite phone may be your only option.
reference: Long ol ples we i stap longwe, we i no gat 'cell phone coverage', em yu ken yusim wanpela 'satellite phone' tasol.

source: A satellite phone is not generally a replacement for a mobile phone, as you have to be outdoors with clear line of sight to the satellite to make a phone call.
reference: Wanpela 'satellite phone' em i no inap long kisim ples bilong wanpela mobail fon, bikos yu bai nid long sanap autsait na yu mas lukim olsem 'satellite' i stap ples klia na yu ken ring.

source: The service is frequently used by shipping, including pleasure craft, as well as expeditions who have remote data and voice needs.
reference: Dispela sevis em planti taim ol sip i save yusim, na ol 'pleasure craft', wantaim ol 'expedition' husat i gat nid long yusim 'remote data' na 'remote voice'.

source: Your local telephone service provider should be able to give more information about connecting to this service.
reference: Lokel 'telephone service provider' bilong yu bai inap long givim moa infomesen long yu long hau yu ken konekt long dispela sevis.

source: An increasingly more popular option for those planning a gap-year is to travel and learn.
reference: Wanpela samting we planti manmeri husat i wok long mekim plen long i gat 'gap-year' i wok long mekim em long travel na lainim samting.

source: This is especially popular with school leavers, allowing them to take a year out before university, without compromising their education.
reference: Dispela em i planti ol lain i lusim skul i wok long mekim, dispela em i mekim ol i kisim wanpela yia long malolo bipo long ol i go long univesiti, na ol i no stopim skul bilong ol.

source: In many cases, enrolling on a gap-year course abroad can actually improve your chances of moving into higher education back in your home country.
reference: Planti taim, pasin bilong enrol na go long wanpela 'gap-year course' long narapela kantru em i ken mekim ol sans bilong yu long muv i go long bikpela skul long kantri bilong yu yet i go antap.

source: Typically there will be a tuition fee to enroll in these educational programs.
reference: Planti taim bai i gat skul fi long enrol long ol dispela edukesen progrem.

source: Finland is a great boating destination. The "Land of a thousand lakes" has thousands of islands too, in the lakes and in the coastal archipelagos.
reference: Finland em i wanpela gutpela kantri bilong raun long bot. Dispela "'Ples bilong tausen raunwara'" i gat planti tausen ailen insait long ol raunwara na long ol solwara.

source: In the archipelagos and lakes you do not necessarily need a yacht.
reference: Long ol liklik ailan na ol raunwara, yu bai no inap nidim wanpela 'yatch'.

source: Although the coastal archipelagos and the biggest lakes are indeed big enough for any yacht, smaller boats or even a kayak offer a different experience.
reference: Ol liklik ailan long solwara na nambawan bikpela raunwara i inap long wanpela 'yatch' o liklik bot o 'kayak' we i ken givim ol narapela we bilong kisim amamas.

source: Boating is a national pastime in Finland, with a boat to every seven or eight people.
reference: 'Boating' o pasin bilong raun long bot em i wanpela samting we olgeta manmeri long Finland i save laik long mekim. Ol i gat planti bot, wanpela bot em i inap long 7-pela o 8-pela manmeri.

source: This is matched by Norway, Sweden and New Zealand, but otherwise quite unique (e.g. in the Netherlands the figure is one to forty).
reference: Dispela em i wankain long Norway, Sweden na New Zealand, tasol em i narakain liklik (eksampel long Netherlands, dispela namba em i 40).

source: Most of the distinct Baltic Cruises feature an extended stay in St. Petersburg, Russia.
reference: Planti bilong ol Baltic Cruises i save larim ol lain i stap longpela taim long St. Petersburg, Russia.

source: This means you can visit the historic city for a couple of full days while returning and sleeping on the ship at night.
reference: Dispela em i min olsem yu ken lukim siti we i gat histori bilong en long sampela de taim yu kam bek long nait na slip long sip.

source: If you only go ashore using shipboard excursions you will not need a separate visa (as of 2009).
reference: Sapos yu go sua long sait bilong wara wantaim ol 'shipboard excursion' yu bai no gat nid long i gat wanpela narapela visa (dispela em i stat long 2009).

source: Some cruises feature Berlin, Germany in the brochures. As you can see from the map above Berlin is no where near the sea and a visit to the city is not included in the price of the cruise.
reference: Sampela bilong ol 'cruise' i save tok olsem ol i karim ol lain i go long Berlin, Germany insait long ol 'brochure' bilong ol. Olsem yu ken lukim long mep (map) i stap antap Berlin i no stap klostu long solwara na wanpela visit long siti em i no stap insait long prais bilong 'cruise'.

source: Travelling by plane can be a scary experience for people of all ages and backgrounds, particularly if they've not flown before or have experienced a traumatic event.
reference: Pasin bilong flai long balus em i ken wanpela ekspiriens wei mekim ol manmeri i gat kainkain krismas na i kam long kainkain ples i pret, bikpela moa em sapos ol i no bin flai bipo o i bin stap insait long wanpela samting nogut we i kamap long balus bipo.

source: It is not something to be ashamed of: it is no different from the personal fears and dislikes of other things that very many people have.
reference: Em i no samting bilong sem long en: em i no narakain long ol narapela samting ol manmeri i save pret long em na ol narapela samting ol planti manmeri i save les long en.

source: For some, understanding something about how aircraft work and what happens during a flight may help to overcome a fear which is based on the unknown or on not being in control.
reference: Long sampela manmeri, pasin bilong klia gut long wanem wanpela balus i save wok na wanem samting i save kamap taim balus i ron i ken helpim ol long daunim ol pret we i save kamap long ol samting we ol i no save long en o ol i pilim olsem ol i no kontrolim.

source: Courier companies are well paid for delivering things quickly. Frequently, time is very important with business documents, merchandise or spare parts for an urgent repair.
reference: Ol 'courier' kampani em ol i save baim ol gut long bringim ol samting hariap. Planti taim, em i impoten long mekim dispela wantaim ol dokumen bilong bisnis, ol 'merchandise' o 'spare parts' long mekim wanpela ripea (repair) hariap.

source: On some routes, the larger companies have their own planes, but for other routes and smaller firms there was a problem.
reference: Long wanpela 'route', ol bikpela kampani i gat ol balus bilong ol yet, tasol long ol narapela 'route' na long ol liklik bisnis i bin i gat wanpela problem.

source: If they sent things by air freight, on some routes it may have taken days to get through unloading and customs.
reference: Sapos ol i salim samting long kago balus, long sampela 'route' em bai kisim planti de long go insait long 'unloading' na 'customs'.

source: The only way to get it through faster was to send it as checked luggage. Airline regulations will not allow them to send luggage without a passenger, which is where you come in.
reference: Wanpela we tasol bilong salim samting hariap em long salim em olsem wanpela 'checked luggage', Ol lo bilong 'airline' bai i no inap long ol bek we i no gat pasindia, na dispela hap em yu bai helpim.

source: The obvious way of flying in first or business class is to fork out a thick wad of money for the privilege (or, better yet, get your company to do it for you).
reference: Isipela we bilong ron long balus insait long namba wan klas o bisnis klas em long yusim bikpela mani long kisim dispela naispela sevis (o, i gutpela moa, larim kampani bilong yu i yusim dispela mani long yu).

source: However, this does not come cheap: as rough rules of thumb, you can expect to pay up to four times the normal economy fare for business, and eleven times for first class!
reference: Tasol, dispela i no save kam long liklik prais: planti taim, yu ken baim prais we i go antap foapela taim moa long prais bilong ekonomi klas long kisim bisnis klas tiket, na yu bai baim prais we i go antap 11-pela taim moa long kisim tiket bilong namba wan klas.

source: Generally speaking, there is no point in even looking for discounts for business or first-class seats on direct flights from A to B.
reference: I no gat as bilong askim long diskaun prais long bisnis klas o namba wan klas long ol 'direct flights', long wanpela ples i go long narapela ples.

source: Airlines know well that there is a certain core group of flyers who are willing to pay top dollar for the privilege of getting somewhere fast and in comfort, and charge accordingly.
reference: Ol balus kampani i save gut tru olsem i gat wanpela grup bilong ron long balus we i save laik long baim bikpela mani long i go hariap long wanpela hap na kisim gutpela sevis insait long balus, olsem na ol i save sasim dispela kain prais.

source: The capital of Moldova is Chişinău. The local language is Romanian, but Russian is widely used.
reference: Kapitol bilong Moldova em Chişinău. Lokel tok ples bilong dispela ples em Romanian, tasol ol i save yusim Russian long planti hap.

source: Moldova is a multi-ethnic republic that has suffered from ethnic conflict.
reference: Moldova em i wanpela 'republic' we i gat kainkain hauslain we i bin kisim taim nogut long kros na pait i kamap namel long ol hauslain.

source: In 1994, this conflict led to the creation of the self-proclaimed Transnistria Republic in eastern Moldova, which has its own government and currency but is not recognised by any UN member country.
reference: Long 1994, dispela kros na pait i bin mekim na wanpela kantri ol i kolim Transnistria Republic i kamap long 'eastern' Moldova, we i gat gavman na karensi (currency) bilong em yet, tasol ol i no luksave long em olsem wanpela UN memba kantri.

source: Economic links have been re-established between these two parts of Moldova despite the failure in political negotiations.
reference: Ol stretim bek gen ol 'economic link' namel long dispela tupela hap bilong Moldova maski ol toktok bilong politiks i no kamap gut.

source: The major religion in Moldova is Orthodox Christian.
reference: Nambawan lotu long Moldova em Orthodox Christian.

source: İzmir is the third largest city in Turkey with a population of around 3.7 million, the second biggest port after Istanbul, and a very good transport hub.
reference: Izmir em i namba tri bikpela siti insait long Turkey wantaim wanpela populesen long kain olsem 3.7 milien, namba tu bikpela port bihainim long Istanbul, na em i wanpela bikpela senta bilong trenspot (transport).

source: Once the ancient city of Smyrna, it is now a modern, developed, and busy commercial center, set around a huge bay and surrounded by mountains.
reference: Bipo em i bin wanpela siti ol i kolim Smyrna, nau em i kamap olsem siti bilong tude, em i divelop pinis, na planti wok bisnis i save kamap long en, em i stap raunim wanpela basis na ol maunten i raunim em.

source: The broad boulevards, glass-fronted buildings and modern shopping centers are dotted with traditional red-tiled roofs, the 18th century market, and old mosques and churches, although the city has an atmosphere more of Mediterranean Europe than traditional Turkey.
reference: Ol bikpela rot, ol bilding i gat glas long fran na ol stoa bilong tude em ol i gat ol 'red-tiled roof' we i tredisinel, '18th century' maket, na ol olpela 'mosque' na sios, tasol dispela siti em olsem wanpela siti bilong Mediterranean Europe na i no Turkey bilong bipo.

source: The village of Haldarsvík offer views of the nearby island Eysturoy and has an unusual octagonal church.
reference: Dispela liklik ples Haldarsvík em yu ken stap long em na lukluk i go long ailan bilong Eysturoy we i stap klostu na em i gat wanpela narapela kain sios we seip (shape) bilong em i olsem 'octagon'.

source: In the churchyard, there are interesting marble sculptures of doves over some tombs.
reference: Long arere bilong dispela sios, i gat ol narapela kain stetu (statue) bilong ol balus pisin we ol i wokim long 'marble' na i stap antap long sampela ston.

source: It's worth half an hour to stroll about the intriguing village.
reference: Yu bai no inap westim taim bilong yu long wokabaut hap aua i go long dispela liklik ples we i gat ol narapela kain samting.

source: To the north and within easy reach is the romantic and fascinating town of Sintra and which was made famous to foreigners after a glowing account of its splendours recorded by Lord Byron.
reference: Long hap not na i stap long ples we isi long go long en em dispela ol i kolim Sintra we i gat histori na i nais tru na planti ol lain long narapela kantri i save long en taim ol ritim toktok bilong Lord Byron long ol naispela samting i stap long em.

source: Scotturb Bus 403 travels regularly to Sintra, stopping at Cabo da Roca.
reference: Scotturb Bus 403 i save ron olgeta taim i go long Sintra, na stop long Cabo da Roca.

source: Also to the north visit the great Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima (Shrine), a place of worldwide famous Marian apparitions.
reference: Na tu long hap not yu ken go Sanctuary of Our Lady of Fatima (Shrine), wanpela ples we olgeta lain i save long en bikos long ol stori bilong Maria mama bilong Jisas i kamap long en.

source: Please remember that you are essentially visiting a mass grave site, as well as a site that has an almost incalculable meaning to a significant portion of the world's population.
reference: Plis tingim olsem yu bai lukim wanpela bikpela matmat we ol i planim planti lain long en, tu dispela ples em i gat bikpela mining long planti manmeri long dispela graun.

source: There are still many men and women alive who survived their time here, and many more who had loved ones who were murdered or worked to death there, Jews and non-Jews alike.
reference: I gat planti manmeri i stap laip yet husat i bin stap long dispela taim, na planti lain moa husat i gat ol lain we ol laikim i bin dai o ol i bin wok go na dai long hap, ol Jew na ol lain i no Jew wantaim.

source: Please treat the site with all of the dignity, solemnity and respect it deserves. Do not make jokes about the Holocaust or Nazis.
reference: Plis soim bikpela rispek long dispela ples. No ken mekim ol tok pilai long Holocaust o Nazis.

source: Do not deface the site by marking or scratching graffiti into structures.
reference: No ken bagarapim dispela ples taim yu put mak ol skrapim 'graffiti' i go insait long ol samting.

source: Barcelona's official languages are Catalan and Spanish. About a half prefer to speak Catalan, a vast majority understands it, and virtually everyone knows Spanish.
reference: Ol nambawan tok ples bilong Barcelona em Catalan na Spanish. Klostu hap namba bilong ol lain i stap long hap i save tok ples Catalan, bikpela namba moa i save long em, na klostu olgeta lain i save long Spanish.

source: However, most signs are indicated only in Catalan because it is established by law as the first official language.
reference: Tasol, planti bilong ol sain i tok long tok ples Catalan tasol bikos lo i tok olsem em i nambawan bikpela tok ples bilong dispela hap.

source: Yet, Spanish is also widely used in public transport and other facilities.
reference: Tasol, ol i save yusim tok ples Spanish long ol pablik trenspot na ol narapela hap.

source: Regular announcements in the Metro are made only in Catalan, but unplanned disruptions are announced by an automated system in a wide variety of languages including Spanish, English, French, Arabic and Japanese.
reference: Ol tok save insait long Metro em ol i save mekim long tok ples Catalan tasol, tasol ol samting we ol i no plen long mekim em wanpela 'automatic' sistem i save mekim dispela tok save long ol kainkain tok ples olsem Spanish, English, French, Arabic na Japanese.

source: Parisians have a reputation for being egocentric, rude and arrogant.
reference: Ol lain long Paris i gat nem long soim pasin we i no gutpela kain olsem tingim ol yet, i no mekim gutpela pasin, ting olsem ol i impoten moa long ol narapela.

source: While this is often only an inaccurate stereotype, the best way to get along in Paris still is to be on your best behavior, acting like someone who is "bien élevé" (well brought up). It will make getting about considerably easier.
reference: Maski dispela em i wanpela 'stereotype' we i no stret, nambawan we long stap long Paris em long mekim ol gutpela pasin, ekt olsem wanpela man o meri husat i "bien élevé" (papamama bilong em i skulim em gut). Em bai mekim isi long i go i kam long Paris.

source: Parisians' abrupt exteriors will rapidly evaporate if you display some basic courtesies.
reference: Ol pasin bilong ol lain long Paris bai senis hariap tru sapos yu soim sampela gutpela pasin.

source: The Plitvice Lakes national park is heavily forested, mainly with beech, spruce, and fir trees, and features a mixture of Alpine and Mediterranean vegetation.
reference: Plitvice Lakes nesinel park em i gat planti bikpela diwai, planti bilong ol dispela diwai em 'beech', 'spruce', na ol 'fir' diwai, na em i gat miks Alpine na Mediterranean plent (plant).

source: It has a notably wide variety of plant communities, due to its range of microclimates, differing soils and varying levels of altitude.
reference: Em i gat kainkain ol plent (plant), bikos long kainkain 'microclimate' bilong en, ol kainkain graun na level bilong graun em i stap long en.

source: The area is also home to an extremely wide variety of animal and bird species.
reference: Dispela eria em i ples bilong ol kainkain animal na pisin.

source: Rare fauna such as the European brown bear, wolf, eagle, owl, lynx, wild cat and capercaillie can be found there, along with many more common species
reference: Ol animal we i no stap long planti hap kain olsem 'European brown bear', 'wolf', 'owl', 'lynx', 'wild cat', na 'capercaillie' i stap long hap, wantaim ol narapela animal we i stap long planti hap.

source: While visiting the monasteries, women are required to wear skirts covering the knees and have their shoulders covered, too.
reference: Taim yu go lukim ol 'monastery', ol meri i mas werim ol sket we i karamapim ni (knee) na tu, ol i mas karamapim ol solda (shoulder) bilong ol.

source: Most of the monasteries do provide wraps for women who come unprepared, but if you bring your own, especially one with bright colors, you'll get a smile from the monk or nun at the entrance.
reference: Planti bilong ol 'monastery' i save givim ol laplap long ol meri we i no redi na i kam, tasol sapos yu bringim laplap bilong yu, gutpela moa long bringim wanpela we i gat ol brait (bright) kala, yu bai kisim wanpela smail long wanpela 'monk' o wanpela 'nun' long dua bilong go insait.

source: Along the same line, men are required to wear trousers covering the knees.
reference: Wankain taim yu, ol man i mas werim ol trausers we i karamapim ni (knee).

source: This too can be borrowed from the stock at the entrance but that clothing isn't washed after every user so you may not feel comfortable wearing these skirts. One size fits all for men!
reference: Dispela tu em ol i boroim (borrow) long ol trausers i stap long dua bilong i go insait, tasol dispela klos em ol i no save wasim bihain long wanpela meri i werim olsem yu bai no inap pilim gut long werimg ol dispela sket. Long ol man em wanpela sais i ken inapim olgeta man!

source: Majorcan cuisine, like that of similar zones in the Mediterranean, is based on bread, vegetables and meat (specially pork), and uses olive oil throughout.
reference: 'Majorcan cuisine', kain olsem ol wankain eria insait long Mediterranean, i save yusim bret, ol gaden kaikai na mit (planti taim em mit bilong pik), na i save yusim 'olive oil' long olgeta samting.

source: A simple popular dinner, especially during the summer, is the Pa amb Oli: Bread with olive oil, tomato, and any available condiments such as cheese, tunafish, etc.
reference: Wanpela kaikai bilong avinun we planti lain i save laikim, em ol i save laikim long mekim long taim bilong sama (summer), em i Pa amb Oli: Bret wantaim 'olive oil', tomato, na wanem samting i stap bilong givim fleiva kain olsem 'cheese', tuna pis na kain olsem.

source: All nouns, alongside the word Sie for you, always begin with a capital letter, even in the middle of a sentence.
reference: Olgeta 'noun', we i stap wantaim dispela wod Sie bilong yu, ol i save stat wantaim wanpela kapitol leta, maski em i stap insait long namel bilong sentens (sentence).

source: This is an important way to distinguish between some verbs and objects.
reference: Dispela em i impoten we bilong luksave long wanem em ol 'verb' na wanem em ol 'object'.

source: It also arguably makes reading easier, though writing is somewhat complicated by the need to find out whether a verb or adjective is used in a substantivized form.
reference: Na tu, em i save mekim pasin bilong rit i isi moa, maski pasin bilong rait em i hap liklik taim yu laik painimaut sapos wanpela 'verb' o 'adjective' em ol i yusim insait long 'substantivized form' bilong em.

source: Pronunciation is relatively easy in Italian since most words are pronounced exactly how they are written
reference: 'Pronunciation' em i tok ples Italian bikos planti bilong ol wod em ol i save kolim olsem hau ol i raitim.

source: The main letters to watch out for are c and g, since their pronunciation varies based on the following vowel.
reference: Ol leta bilong tingim em 'c' na 'g', bikos 'pronunciation' bilong ol i save senis bihainim 'vowel' we i kam bihain long ol.

source: Also, make sure to pronounce r and rr differently: caro means dear, whereas carro means chariot.
reference: Na tu, yu mas sekim olsem yu kolim 'r' na 'rr' narakain: 'caro' em i min olsem dia, na 'carro' em i min olsem 'chariot'.

source: Persian has a relatively easy and mostly regular grammar.
reference: Perian tok ples em i isi na grama (grammar) bilong em i isi long bihainim.

source: Therefore, reading this grammar primer would help you learn much about Persian grammar and understand phrases better.
reference: Olsem na, taim yu ritim dispela 'grammar primer' em bai helpim yu tru long grama (grammar) bilong tok ples Persia na yu ken save gut long ol hap toktok.

source: Needless to say, if you know a Romance language, it will be easier for you to learn Portuguese.
reference: Em i klia tru olsem, sapos yu save long wanpela Romance tok ples, em bai isi moa long yu long lainim Portuguese.

source: However, people who know a little Spanish may hastily conclude that Portuguese is close enough that it need not be studied separately.
reference: Tasol, ol manmeri husat i save liklik log Spanish i ken ting olsem Portuguese em i klostu wankain olsem Spanish na i no gat nid long stadi long em.

source: Pre-modern observatories are usually obsolete today, and remain as museums, or sites of education.
reference: Ol 'observatory' bilong taim bipo em ol i no yusim moa long tude, na nau ol i stap olsem ol 'museum', o ples bilong edukesen.

source: As light pollution in their heyday was not the kind of problem it is today, they are usually located in cities or at campuses, easier to reach than those built in modern times.
reference: 'Light pollution' i no bin wanpela problem long mak we em i stap long en tude, ol i save stap long ol siti o long ol skul, na i isi long go long en na i no olsem ol dispela we ol i bildim long tude.

source: Most modern research telescopes are enormous facilities in remote areas with favorable atmospheric conditions.
reference: Planti ol 'research telescope' em ol i bikpela fesiliti (facilty) we i stap long ol longwe ples long ol gutpela 'atmospheric condition'.

source: Cherry blossom viewing, known as hanami, has been a part of Japanese culture since the 8th century.
reference: Pasin bilong lukluk long 'cherry blossom', we ol i kolim olsem hanami, em i pat bilong kalsa bilong Japanese stat long '8th century' na i kam.

source: The concept came from China where plum blossoms were the flower of choice.
reference: Dispela pasin em i kam long Chain we ol i save lukluk long ol 'plum blossom'.

source: In Japan, the first cherry blossom parties were hosted by the emperor only for himself and other members of the aristocracy around the Imperial Court.
reference: Long Japan, empera (emperor) i save holim ol pati bilong ol namba wan 'cherry blossom' pati bilong em yet na ol memba bilong 'aristocracy' long Imperial Court.

source: Plants look their best when in a natural environment, so resist the temptation to remove even "just one" specimen.
reference: Ol plent (plant) i save luk nais tru insait long wel envaironmen bilong ol, olsem an no ken traim long rausim "wanpela tasol" na kisim olsem ‘specimen’.

source: If visiting a formally arranged garden, collecting "specimens" is also going to get you ejected, without discussion.
reference: Sapos yu go long wanpela gaden we ol i stretim bilong ol lalin i kam na lukim, pasin bilong kolektim ol "'specimen’" bai mekim na ol i rausim yu, na ol i no inap toktok planti.

source: Singapore is generally an extremely safe place to be and very easy to navigate, and you can buy almost anything after arriving.
reference: Singapore em i seif tru na isi long i go i kam, na yu ken baim kainkain samting taim yu kamap long hap.

source: But being placed in the "high tropics" just a few degrees north of equator you will need to deal with both heat (always) and strong sun (when the sky is clear, more rarely).
reference: Tasol bikos em i stap long "‘high tropics’" sampela ‘degree’ not long ‘equator’ yu bai nid long stap insait long hot (olgeta taim) na strongpela san (taim san i klia, dispela i no save kamap planti taim).

source: There are also a few buses going north to Hebron, the traditional burial place of the Biblical patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and their wives.
reference: Na tu, i gat sampela bas (bus) i save go long hap not long Hebron, dispela em i ples we ol i planim ol papa bilong bilip insait long Baibel Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, na ol meri bilong ol.

source: Check that the bus you are thinking of taking goes into Hebron and not just to the nearby Jewish settlement of Kiryat Arba.
reference: Sekim sapos ol bas (bus) yu tingting long kisim em bai go insait long Hebron na i no go klostu long setelmen (settlement) bilong ol Jewish long Kiryat Arba.

source: Inland waterways can be a good theme to base a holiday around.
reference: Ol wara we i stap insait long ples em ol gutpela samting bilong mekim holide long en.

source: For example visiting castles in the Loire Valley, the Rhine valley or taking a cruise to interesting cites on the Danube or boating along the Erie Canal.
reference: Eksampel, taim yu go lukim ol kasol (castle) insait long Loire Valley, Rhine valley o kisim wanpela ‘cruise’ i go long ol naispela siti long Danube o ‘boating’ long Erie Canal.

source: They also define routes for popular hiking and cycling trails.
reference: Na tu, ol i save makim ol ‘route’ we planti lain i save go wokabaut o ‘cycling’ long em.

source: Christmas is one of the most important holidays of Christianity, and is celebrated as the birthday of Jesus.
reference: Krismas em i wanpela bilong ol impoten holide bilong Christianity, na ol i save amamasim dispela de olsem bonde bilong Jisas.

source: Many of the traditions surrounding the holiday have been adopted also by non-believers in Christian countries and non-Christians around the world.
reference: Planti bilong ol pasin we ol lain i save mekim long dispela holide em ol lain i no Kristen na ol kantri we i no Kristen kantri i save bihainim long olgeta hap bilong graun.

source: There's a tradition to pass the Easter night awake at some exposed point to see the sunrise.
reference: I gat tredisen (tradition) i bilong no ken slip long Easter nait na stap long wanpela ples klia long lukim san i kirap.

source: There are of course Christian theological explanations for this tradition, but it may well be a pre-Christian Spring and Fertility ritual.
