source: Planti bilong ol tingting long mining bilong 'technological determinism' i gat tupela bikpela tingting insait long ol: wanpela em i olsem divelopmen bilong teknoloji i bihainim wanpela rot we kalsa na politiks i no inap long senisim, na narapela em i olsem teknoloji i kamapim "senis" long ol komuniti bikos dispela ol pasin na tingting em ol manmeri i bon wantaim na teknoloji i no kamapim.
reference: Most interpretations of technological determinism share two general ideas: that the development of technology itself follows a path largely beyond cultural or political influence, and that technology in turn has "effects" on societies that are inherent, rather than socially conditioned.

source: Eksampel em, yumi i ken tok olsem kar i mekim na ol manmeri i kamapim ol rot.
reference: For example, one might say that the motor car necessarily leads to the development of roads.

source: Tasol, wanpela rot we i go long olgeta hap bilong kantri em bai i westim mani bikos i gat sampela kar tasol, olsem na ol nupela rot bilong wokim kar i kamap bilong daunim prais bilong kar.
reference: However, a nationwide road network is not economically viable for just a handful of cars, so new methods of production are developed to reduce the cost of car ownership.

source: Taim planti manmeri i gat kar, planti eksiden i ken kamap long ol rot, dispela bai i mekim na ol lain bai i kamapim ol nupela teknik insait long heltkea long stretim ol bagarap long bodi.
reference: Mass car ownership also leads to a higher incidence of accidents on the roads, which leads to the invention of new techniques in healthcare for repairing damaged bodies.

source: Romanticicm i bin gat wanpela bikpela hap tingting bilong 'cultural determinism' insait long em, dispela tingting em i kam long 'writer' olsem Goethe, Fichte, na Schlegel.
reference: Romanticism had a large element of cultural determinism, drawn from writers such as Goethe, Fichte, and Schlegel.

source: Long sait bilong Romanticisim, ples we ol man i kam long en i save mekim ol i gat wanpela kain pasin, na bihain long sampela taim ol kastom na ol kalsa we i go wantaim ples we ol i stap long en i save kamap, na ol dispela, bai soim wanem pasin em i pasin bilong ol manmeri long dispela ples, dispela em i gutpela moa long ol lo we ol lain i kisim nating long narapela hap na i kam yusim.
reference: In the context of Romanticism, the geography molded individuals, and over time customs and culture related to that geography arose, and these, being in harmony with the place of the society, were better than arbitrarily imposed laws.

source: Long dispela rot, Paris i kamap olsem nambawan ples bilong ol kainkain stail bilong klos long dispela graun long tude, tasol Constantinople em i stap olsem nambawan siti bilong kainkain stail klos long taim bilong 'middle ages' long Europe.
reference: In the manner that Paris is known as the fashion capital of the contemporary world, Constantinople was regarded as the fashion capital of feudal Europe.

source: Planti lain manmeri i save long em bikos em i asples bilong gutpela laipstail na em i bin stat long 400 A.D na pinis long kain taim olsem 1100 A.D.
reference: Its renown for being an epicenter of luxury began in about 400 A.D. and lasted up until about 1100 A.D.

source: Long 'twelfth century', velu (value) bilong em i go daun bikos ol Crusaders i bin kam bek na ol i karim ol presen kain olsem ol silk na spais. Velu bilong ol dispela samting i antap moa long velu bilong ol samting we i stap long ol maket bilong ol Byzantine.
reference: Its status declined during the twelfth century mainly due to the fact that Crusaders had returned bearing gifts such as silks and spices that were valued more than what Byzantine markets offered.

source: Long dispela taim, ol i kisim dispela nem 'Fashion Capital' long siti Constantinople na givim i go long siti Paris.
reference: It was at this time that the transfer of the title of Fashion Capital from Constantinople to Paris was made.

source: Gothic style i kamap bikpela long taim namel long '10th - 11th century' na '14 century'.
reference: Gothic style peaked in the period between the 10th - 11th centuries and the 14th century.

source: Pastaim dispela dres i bihainim stail bilong Byzantine kalsa bilong is.
reference: At the beginning dress was heavily influenced by the Byzantine culture in the east.

source: Tasol, bikos pasin bilong salim toktok i go i kam i save kisim taim, ol stail bilong wes bai stap bihain long 25 i go long 30-pela yia.
reference: However, due to the slow communication channels, styles in the west could lag behind by 25 to 30 year.

source: taim i go long pinis bilong ol Middle Ages 'western' Europe i stat long kamapim ol stail bilong ol yet. Wanpela bilong ol bikpela divelopmen we i kamap long dispela taim bikos long ol 'crusades' em ol manmeri i stat long yusim ol baten long pasim ol klos.
reference: towards the end of the Middle Ages western Europe began to develop their own style. one of the biggest developments of the time as a result of the crusades people began to use buttons to fasten clothing.

source: 'Subistence agriculture' em i agrikalsa (agriculture) we ol lain i mekim long kamapim kaikai long mak bilong famili bilong ol.
reference: Subsistence agriculture is agriculture carried out for the production of enough food to meet just the needs of the agriculturalist and his/her family.

source: 'Subsistence agriculture' em i wanpela sistem we i no yusim planti samting, i no yusim marasin, i yusim sit we ol i kisim long kaikai we i gro long dispela rijen na ol i bungim dispela ol pasin wantaim pasin bilong mekim 'crop rotation' o ol narapela isipela teknik (technique) bilong mekim kaikai i kamap planti.
reference: Subsistence agriculture is a simple, often organic, system using saved seed native to the ecoregion combined with crop rotation or other relatively simple techniques to maximize yield.

source: Bipo planti fama (farmer) i save mekim 'subsistence agriculture' na dispela pasin em i stap yet long planti kantri we i no divelop yet.
reference: Historically most farmers were engaged in subsistence agriculture and this is still the case in many developing nations.

source: Ol 'subculture' i save mekim ol lain i gat wankain tingting na i pilim olsem ol stendet bilong 'society' i no bilong ol na taim ol i bung wantaim ol i kamapim wanpela 'identity' bilong ol yet.
reference: Subcultures bring together like-minded individuals who feel neglected by societal standards and allow them to develop a sense of identity.

source: Ol 'subculture' em ol i ken narakain narakain bikos long krismas, ples ol lain i kam long en, level bilong ol insait long komuniti, ples ol i stap long en, na 'gender' bilong ol.
reference: Subcultures can be distinctive because of the age, ethnicity, class, location, and/or gender of the members.

source: Ol samting we i mekim ol 'subculture' i narakain narakain em i ken tok ples, samting we i nais, lotu, politiks, 'sexual', ples, o ol dispela samting i bung wantaim.
reference: The qualities that determine a subculture as distinct may be linguistic, aesthetic, religious, political, sexual, geographical, or a combination of factors.

source: Ol memba bilong wanpela 'subculture' i ken soim olsem ol i memba bilong em taim ol i yusim wanpela kain stail, kain olsem klos ol i werim, pasin ol i gat long en, na toktok ol i yusim.
reference: Members of a subculture often signal their membership through a distinctive and symbolic use of style, which includes fashions, mannerisms, and argot.

source: Wanpela bilong ol metod (method) we planti lain i save yusim long soim olsem 'socialization' em i impoten em ol i save yusim stori bilong ol pikinini we i bin 'socialized' bikos ol papamama i no bisi long ol, ol samting nogut i kamap long laip bilong ol, o ol lain i mekim nogut long ol taim ol i wok long gro.
reference: One of the most common methods used to illustrate the importance of socialization is to draw upon the few unfortunate cases of children who were, through neglect, misfortune, or wilful abuse, not socialized by adults while they were growing up.

source: Dispela kain ol pikinini em ol i save kolim ol olsem "'feral" o wel pikinini. Sampela 'feral' pikinini em sampela lain (planti taim em i papamam bilong ol yet) i save mekim ol i stap long haus tasol; long sampela taim ol papamama i no bisi long dispela pikinini bikos em i gat samting i rong long het bilong em o long bodi bilong em.
reference: Such children are called "feral" or wild. Some feral children have been confined by people (usually their own parents); in some cases this child abandonment was due to the parents' rejection of a child's severe intellectual or physical impairment.

source: Ol 'feral' pikinini em ol sampela bikpela bagarap o 'trauma' i bin kamap long ol bipo long ol i ranawe o papamama i no bisi long ol.
reference: Feral children may have experienced severe child abuse or trauma before being abandoned or running away.

source: Ol narapela 'feral' pikinini em ol lain i tok olsem ol animal long bus i lukautim ol; sampela em ol lain i tok olsem ol i stap ol yet long bus.
reference: Others are alleged to have been brought up by animals; some are said to have lived in the wild on their own.

source: Taim ol animal i lukautim ol dispela pikinini, ol dispela 'feral' pikinini save soim ol pasin (we ol inap long mek) we i wankain olsem pasin bilong dispela animal we i lukautim ol, kain olsem pret o i no save wanem samting bilong mekim taim em i stap wantaim ol narapela manmeri.
reference: When completely brought up by non-human animals, the feral child exhibits behaviors (within physical limits) almost entirely like those of the particular care-animal, such as its fear of or indifference to humans.

source: Maski, 'project based learning' i mekim pasin bilong lainim samting i kamap isi moa na i kirapim intres (interest), 'scaffolding' em i mekim moa samting.
reference: While project based learning should make learning easier and more interesting, scaffolding goes a step beyond.

source: 'Scaffolding' em i no wanpela metod (method) bilong lainim samting tasol em i wanpela helpim we i save givim sapot long ol wanwan sumatin husat i wok long lainim wanpela nupela samting kain olsem yusim wanpela nupela komputa o statim wanpela nupela projek (project).
reference: Scaffolding is not a method of learning but rather an aid that provides support to individuals whom are undergoing a new learning experience such as using a new computer program or beginning a new project.

source: Ol 'scaffold' em ol i ken kamap long komputa o em i ken tru insait long klasrum, narapela we bilong tok em i olsem, wanpela tisa em wanpela 'scaffold', tasol wanpela 'paperclip man' insait long Microsoft Office em tu i wanpela 'scaffold'.
reference: Scaffolds can be both virtual and real, in other words, a teacher is a form of scaffold but so is the little paperclip man in Microsoft Office.

source: Ol 'scaffold' long komputa em ol i stap insait long ol 'software' na wok bilong ol em long askim kwesten, tok save long wanem samting bilong mekim, na tok klia long ol rot bilong mekim samting we i hat long ol sumatin long mekim long save bilong ol yet.
reference: Virtual Scaffolds are internalized in the software and are meant to question, prompt, and explain procedures that may have been to challenging for the student to handle alone.

source: Ol i save putim ol pikinini long Foster Care bikos long kainkain samting na em i ken 'neglect', i go long 'abuse', na pasin bilong fosim pikinini long mekim samting bilong kisim mani.
reference: Children are placed in Foster Care for a wide variety of reasons that range from neglect, to abuse, and even to extortion.

source: No gat wanpela pikinini i mas stap insait long wanpela envairomen we i no gutpela long ol, i no lukautim ol, na i givim save long ol, tasol ol i save stap insait long kain envairomen olsem.
reference: No child should ever have to grow up in an environment that is not nurturing, caring, and educational, but they do.

source: Mipela i lukim Foster Care System olsem wanpela ples we i seif long ol dispela pikinini.
reference: We perceive the Foster Care System to be a safety zone for these children.

source: 'Foster Care System' bilong mipela em i sapos long givim ol ples we i seif long ol pikinini long i stap, lain we i ken laikim ol na lukautim ol, gutpela helt kea (health care).
reference: Our foster care system is supposed to provide safe homes, loving caregivers, stable education, and reliable health care.

source: Ol 'foster care' i sapos long givim olgeta samting we ol i no bin gat long haus we ol i kisim ol long en.
reference: Foster care is supposed to provide all the necessities that were lacking in the home they were previously taken from.

source: Intanet i save bungim ol pasin bilong toktok long wanwan man o meri wantaim ol pasin bilong toktok long planti manmeri.
reference: The Internet combines elements of both mass and interpersonal communication.

source: Dispela ol pasin bilong intanet i save givim nupela save long wanem we yumi ken yusim ‌intanet na kisim amamas.
reference: The distinct characteristics of the Internet lead to additional dimensions in terms of the uses and gratifications approach.

source: Eksampel em, “'learning” and “socialization'” em ol impoten motivesen (motivation) we i save mekim ol manmeri i laik long yusim intanet (James et al., 1995).
reference: For example, “learning” and “socialization” are suggested as important motivations for Internet use (James et al., 1995).

source: “'Personal involvement'” na “'continuing relationship'” em Eighmey and McCord (1998) i bin luksave olsem ol nupela samting we i kirapim bel bilong yumi long yusim intanet taim ol i mekim stadi long pasin bilong ol manmeri long ol websait.
reference: “Personal involvement” and “continuing relationships” were also identified as new motivation aspects by Eighmey and McCord (1998) when they investigated audience reactions to websites.

source: Pasin bilong yusim vidio rekoding i mekim na ol saveman i painimaut long ol impoten samting long sait bilong mining bilong 'micro-expression', ol muvmen bilong pes we i save kamap insait long sampela 'millisecond'.
reference: The use of video recording has led to important discoveries in the interpretation of micro-expressions, facial movements which last a few milliseconds.

source: Bikpela moa, ol i tok olsem yumi i ken painimaut sapos wanpela man o meri i giaman taim yumi painimaut mining bilong 'micro-expression' bilong em long stretpela we.
reference: In particular, it is claimed that one can detect whether a person is lying by interpreting micro-expressions correctly.

source: Oliver Sacks, insait long pepa we em i raitim The President's Speech, em i soim hau olsem manmeri i no inap long klia gut long mining bilong toktok bikos ol i gat sampela bagarap i kamap long brein (brain) bilong ol tasol ol i klia stret sapos man em i tok tru.
reference: Oliver Sacks, in his paper The President's Speech, indicated how people who are unable to understand speech because of brain damage are nevertheless able to assess sincerity accurately.

source: Em i tok tu olsem ol dispela kain save bilong man long lukim mining bilong pasin bilong ol man em ol animal tu i gat kain olsem ol dok we yumi save lukautim long haus.
reference: He even suggests that such abilities in interpreting human behavior may be shared by animals such as domestic dogs.

source: Ol risets i kamap long 'Twentieth century' i bin soim olsem i gat tupela 'pool' bilong 'genetic variation': wanpela em i hait na wanpela em i stap ples klia.
reference: Twentieth century research has shown that there are two pools of genetic variation: hidden and expressed.

source: 'Mutation' i save mekim nupela 'genetic variation' i kamap, na 'selection' i save rausim em long 'pool' bilong 'expressed variation'.
reference: Mutation adds new genetic variation, and selection removes it from the pool of expressed variation.

source: 'Segregation' na 'recombination' i save mekim dispela 'variation' i go i kam namel long tupela 'pool' insait long wanwan 'generation'.
reference: Segregation and recombination shuffle variation back and forth between the two pools with each generation.

source: Long 'savaana', em i hat long wanpela 'primate' we i gat wanpela 'digestive system' kain olsem bilong ol man long inapim ol 'amino-acid' long ol plent (plant) i stap long hap.
reference: Out on the savanna, it is hard for a primate with a digestive system like that of humans to satisfy its amino-acid requirements from available plant resources.

source: Na moa yet, sapos ol i no inap long mekim dispela ol bikpela samting i ken kamap: 'growth depression', 'malnutrition', na bikpela tru em i dai.
reference: Moreover, failure to do so has serious consequences: growth depression, malnutrition, and ultimately death.

source: Dispela plent (plant) we i stap long dispela hap em ol 'protein' i save stap insait long ol lip na ol bin bilong en, tasol dispela em i hat long ol bel bilong ol 'primate' olsem mipela long brukim i go daun na ol i mas kukim ol pastaim.
reference: The most readily accessible plant resources would have been the proteins accessible in leaves and legumes, but these are hard for primates like us to digest unless they are cooked.

source: Narapela samting, em animal kaikai (ol anis, ol 'termite', na kiau) em ol i isi long kaikai, na tu ol i givim bikpela mak bilong ol 'protein' we i gat olgeta ol 'amino acid' we bodi i nidim.
reference: In contrast, animal foods (ants, termites, eggs) not only are easily digestible, but they provide high-quantity proteins that contain all the essential amino acids.

source: Taim yumi skelim olgeta samting, yumi mas no ken kirap nogut sapos ol tumbuna bilong ol i stretim ol "problem bilong 'protein'" bilong ol long wankain we olsem ol 'chimps' long 'savanna' i save mekim long tude.
reference: All things considered, we should not be surprised if our own ancestors solved their "protein problem" in somewhat the same way that chimps on the savanna do today.

source: 'Sleep interruption' em i pasin bilong kirap namel long slip na go bek long slip sotpela taim bihain (10–60 minit).
reference: Sleep interruption is the process of purposefully awakening during your normal sleep period and falling asleep a short time later (10–60 minutes).

source: Dispela em yu ken mekim taim yu yusim wanpela 'quiet alarm clock' long mekim yu i kirap isi isi na i no kirapim yu wantu.
reference: This can be easily done by using a relatively quiet alarm clock to bring you to consciousness without fully waking you.

source: Sapos yu lukim olsem yu mekim 'reset' long kilok namel long slip bilong yu, yu ken putim em long narapela sait bilong rum, dispela em bai mekim yu i kirap long bed long i go ofim em.
reference: If you find yourself resetting the clock in your sleep, it can be placed on the other side of the room, forcing you to get out of bed to turn it off.

source: Narapela opsen (option) bilong 'biorhythm-based' em bai yu dringim planti wara (kain olsem wara o ti, wanpela 'diuretic') bipo long yu slip, dispela bai fosim yu long kirap na i go pispis.
reference: Other biorhythm-based options involve drinking lots of fluid (particularly water or tea, a known diuretic) prior to sleep, forcing one to get up to urinate.

source: Mak bilong bel isi i stap insait long wanpela man i gat koneksen (connection) tasol em i oposit (opposite) long mak bilong hevi we i stap insait long bodi na spirit bilong wanpela man.
reference: The amount of inner peace a person possesses correlates oppositely to the amount of tension in one’s body and spirit.

source: Taim hevi em i liklik, bai i gat moa positif fos bilong laip i stap. Olgeta man i gat strong long painim bel isi tru na amamas.
reference: The lower the tension, the more positive the life force present. Every person has the potential to find absolute peace and contentment.

source: Olgeta lain i ken kisim 'enlightenment'. Wanpela samting tasol we i sanap namel long dispela as tingting em hevi na 'negativity' bilong yumi yet.
reference: Everyone can achieve enlightenment. The only thing standing in the way of this goal is our own tension and negativity.

source: Tibetan Buddhism em i bihainim ol samting we Buddha i tisim, tasol ol i mekim em i go bikpela moa wantaim 'mahayana path of love' na tu wantaim planti ol teknik (technique) bilong Indian Yoga.
reference: The Tibetan Buddhism is based on the teachings of Buddha, but were extended by the mahayana path of love and by a lot of techniques from Indian Yoga.

source: Insait long as tingting bilong em Tibetan Buddhism em i isi tru. Em i gat Kundalini Yoga, 'meditation' and the rot bilong 'all-embracing love'.
reference: In principle the Tibetan Buddhism is very simple. It consists of Kundalini Yoga, meditation and the path of all-embracing love.

source: Wantaim Kundalini Yoga dispela Kundalini energy ('enlightenment energy') em i save kirap insait long 'yoga postures', eksesais bilong pulim win, ol 'mantra' na 'visualizations'.
reference: With Kundalini Yoga the Kundalini energy (enlightenment energy) is awakened through yoga postures, breathing exercises, mantras and visualizations.

source: Nambawan samting insait long 'Tibetan meditation' em Deity Yoga. Insait long 'visualization' bilong ol kainkain god ol rot bilong eneji i kamap klin, ol 'chakra' i kamap ektif (active) na 'enlightenment' bilong tingting i kamap.
reference: The center of Tibetan meditation is the Deity Yoga. Through the visualization of various deities the energy channels are cleaned, the chakras are activated and the enlightenment consciousness is created.

source: Germany em i stap olsem birua bilong planti lain long World War 2, dispela i mekim na wok bung i kamap namel long USSR na USA. Taim woa i pinis, bam i kamap namel long ol sistem, ol proses (process) na kalsa na dispela i mekim ol kantri i no wok wantaim.
reference: Germany was a common enemy in World War 2, leading to cooperation between the USSR and USA. With the end of the war the clashes of system, process and culture led to the countries falling out.

source: Bihain long tupela yia long pinis bilong woa, ol lain we i bin wok wantaim i kamap olsem ol birua na Cold War i stat.
reference: With two years of the end of the war, the former allies were now enemies and the Cold War began.

source: Dispela woa em bai i stap 40-pela yia na pait i kamap tru tru, long ol ami (army) we i makim ol kantri, long ol ples bilong pait long Africa i go long Asia, long Afghanistan, Cuba na planti narapela ples.
reference: It was to last for the next 40 years and would be fought for real, by proxy armies, on battlefields from Africa to Asia, in Afghanistan, Cuba and many other places.

source: Long September 17, 1939, ol difens (defence) bilong ol Poland em i bruk, na wanpela samting tasol bilong mekim em long go bek na bung gen long 'bridgehead' bilong Romania.
reference: By September 17, 1939, the Polish defense was already broken, and the only hope was to retreat and reorganise along the Romanian bridgehead.

source: Tasol, ol dispela plen (plan) i bagarap long dispela nait, moa long 800,000 soldia bilong Rrd Army bilong Soviet Union i go insait na i kamapim wanpela 'front' long Belarus na wanpela 'front' long Ukraine bihain long ol i go insait na holimpasim ol 'eastern region' bilong Poland na dispela pasin em i no bihaibim Riga Peace Treaty, Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, na ol narapela intanesinel agrimen (agreement) we i kamap namel long tupela kantri na tu, i kamap namel long planti narapela kantri.
reference: However, these plans were rendered obsolete nearly overnight, when over 800,000 soldiers from the Soviet's Union Red Army entered and created the Belarussian and Ukrainian fronts after invading the eastern regions of Poland in violation of the Riga Peace Treaty, the Soviet-Polish Non-Aggression Pact, and other international treaties, both bilateral and multilateral.

source: Pasin bilong yusim sip long muvim ol samting em i nambawan we tru bilong muvim bikpela namba bilong ol kago o ol manmeri long solwara.
reference: Using ships to transport goods is by far the most efficient way to move large amounts of people and goods across oceans.

source: Wok bilong ol 'navy' em i bilong lukim olsem kantri bilong yu em i inap long muvin ol kago na manmeri, na tu, stopim ol birua long muvim ol kago na ol manmeri bilong em.
reference: The job of navies has traditionally been to ensure that your country maintains the ability to move your people and goods, while at the same time, interfering with your enemy's ability to move his people and goods.

source: Wanpela gutpela eksampel we i kamap i no longtaim em dispela kempen (campaign) bilong North Altlantic long WWII. Ol America i wok long traim long muvim ol kago na ol man long Atlantic Ocean long helpim ol Britain.
reference: One of the most noteworthy recent examples of this was the North Atlantic campaign of WWII. The Americans were trying to move men and materials across the Atlantic Ocean to help Britain.

source: Long wankain taim, 'navy' bilong Germany, i yusim ol U-boat, na i wok long traim long stopim dispela long kamap.
reference: At the same time, the German navy, using mainly U-boats, was trying to stop this traffic.

source: Sapos ol Allies i no mekim samting ol i bin mekim, Germany ating bai holimpasim na kisim Britain olsem em i bin mekim long olgeta hap bilong Europe.
reference: Had the Allies failed, Germany probably would have been able to conquer Britain as it had the rest of Europe.

source: Ol man i stat long lukautim meme long kain taim olsem 10,000 yia i go pinis long Zagros Mountains bilong Iran.
reference: Goats seem to have been first domesticated roughly 10,000 years ago in the Zagros Mountains of Iran.

source: Ol kalsa bilong bipo tru na ol 'tribe' i stat long lukautim ol long mekim isi long kisim milk, gras, mit na skin bilong ol.
reference: Ancient cultures and tribes began to keep them for easy access to milk, hair, meat, and skins.

source: Ol meme bilong we ol man i save lukautim em ol i save putim ol long grup we i save raun long ol liklik maunten o ol narapela hap bilong kaikai, planti taim ol 'goatherd' i save was long ol na dispela em ol pikinini ol bikpela man we ol i save kolim olsem 'sheperd'. Ol dispela ol pasin bilong lukautim ol meme em ol i wok long yusim yet long tude.
reference: Domestic goats were generally kept in herds that wandered on hills or other grazing areas, often tended by goatherds who were frequently children or adolescents, similar to the more widely known shepherd. These methods of herding are still used today.

source: Ol Wagonways em ol i bin bildim long England taim bipo long '16th Century'.
reference: Wagonways were built in England as early as the 16th Century.

source: Maski ol 'wagonways' em ol plang we i stap sait sait long rot, ol i mekim ol hos we i pulim ol long spit moa na i pulim bikpela kago long ol rot we i no raf (rough) moa long mak bilong ol rot we i save stap long dispela de.
reference: Although wagonways merely consisted of parallel planks of wood, they allowed horses pulling them to achieve greater speeds and pull larger loads than on the slightly more rough roads of the day.

source: Ol i yusim ol 'crossties' pastaim long holim ol dispela 'track' i stap long wanpela ples. Bihain, nau, ol i luksave olsem ol dispela 'track' bai kamap gutpela moa sapos ol i gat wanpela hap ain i stap antap long ol.
reference: Crossties were introduced fairly early to hold the tracks in place. Gradually, however, it was realised that tracks would be more efficient if they had a stip of iron on the top.

source: Dispela pasin em planti lain i save mekim, tasol em i kamapim 'wear' long plang wil bilong ol 'wagon'.
reference: This became common practice, but the iron caused more wear on the wooden wheels of the wagons.

source: Bihain, ol i senisim ol plang wil wantaim ain wil. Long 1767, ol i wokim ol namba wan 'full-iron rails'.
reference: Eventually, wooden wheels were replaced by iron wheels. In 1767, the first full-iron rails were introduced.

source: Namba wan 'transportation' em wokabaut, ol manmeri i stat long wokabaut long tupela lek tu miline yia i go pinis taim ol Homo Erectus (dispela em i min olsem 'upright man') i kamap.
reference: The first known transportation was walking, humans began walking upright two million years ago with the emergence of Homo Erectus (meaning upright man).

source: Ol lain i stap pastaim, ol Australopithecus i no wokabut long tupela lek olgeta taim.
reference: Their predecessors, the Australopithecus did not walk upright as habitually.

source: Pasin bilong wokabaut long tupela lek em ol i painim long fosil (fossil) bilong ol Australopithecus we i bin stap 4.2-3.9 milien yia i go pinis, tasol ol Sahelanthropus i bin wokabaut long tupela lek long kain taim olsem seven milien yia i go pinis.
reference: Bipedal specializations are found in Australopithecus fossils from 4.2-3.9 million years ago, although Sahelanthropus may have walked on two legs as early as seven million years ago.

source: Yumi ken stat long stap gut wantaim envairomen, yumi ken joinim wanpela 'environmental movement', na yumi ken kamap olsem 'activist' long daunim mak bilong hevi we i ken kamap long bihaintaim.
reference: We can start living more friendly to the environment, we can join to the environmental movement, and we can even be activists in order to reduce the future suffering in some degree.

source: Planti taim dispela em i wankain olsem 'sympomatic treatment'. Tasol, sapos yumi i no laikim wanpela sotpela ansa, orait yumi mas painim as bilong problem, na yumi mas stopim ol.
reference: This is just like symptomatic treatment in many cases. However, if we do not only want a temporary solution, then we should find the root of the problems, and we should deactivate them.

source: Em i is long lukim olsem graun i senis tru bikos long ol nupela save bilong sains (science) na teknoloji (technology), na ol problem i wok long kamap bikpela bikos long planti manmeri na laipstail bilong manmeri long i gat ol kainkain bikpela samting.
reference: It is obvious enough that the world has changed much because of humankind's scientific and technological advancements, and problems have become greater because of overpopulation and mankind's extravagant lifestyle.

source: Bihain long Congress i kisim dispela pepa long Julai 4, wanpela kopi we ol i raitim long han na President of Congress John Hancock na Secretary Charles Thomson tupela i sainim, ol i salim i go long wanpela 'printing shop' bilong John Dunlop we i stap klostu.
reference: After its adoption by Congress on July 4, a handwritten draft signed by the President of Congress John Hancock and the Secretary Charles Thomson was then sent a few blocks away to the printing shop of John Dunlap.

source: Namel long nait kain olsem 150 na 200 kopi em ol i mekim, nau ol i kolim olsem "Dunlap broadsides".
reference: Through the night between 150 and 200 copies were made, now known as "Dunlap broadsides".

source: Namba wan taim ol i ritim dispela dokumen long pablik em John Nixon i ritim autsait long Independence Hall long Julai 8.
reference: The first public reading of the document was by John Nixon in the yard of Independence Hall on July 8.

source: Wanpela em ol i salim i go long George Washington long Julai 6, na em i ritim long ol soldia bilong em long New York long Julai 9. Wanpela kopi i kamap long London long Ogas 10.
reference: One was sent to George Washington on July 6, who had it read to his troops in New York on July 9. A copy reached London on August 10.

source: 25-pela Dunlop broadies em ol i stap yet na ol i olpela kopi tru bilong dispela dokumen. Dispela namba wan dokumen we ol i raitim long han em i bagarap.
reference: The 25 Dunlap broadsides still known to exist are the oldest surviving copies of the document. The original handwritten copy has not survived.

source: Planti bilong ol 'paleontologists' tude i bilip olsem wanpela grup bilong ol dainaso i stap laip yet long tude. Mipela i save kolim ol olsem pisin.
reference: Many paleontologists today believe that one group of dinosaurs survived and is alive today. We call them birds.

source: Planti manmeri i no ting olsem ol i wankain olsem ol dainaso bikos ol i gat 'feather' na ol i ken flai.
reference: Many people don't think about them as dinosaurs because they have feathers and can fly.

source: Tasol i gat planti samting long ol pisin we i luk wankain olsem ol dainaso yet.
reference: But there are a lot of things about birds that still look like a dinosaur.

source: Ol i gat lek we i gat 'scale' na 'claws', ol i save putim kiau, na ol i save wokabaut long tupela lek olsem wanpela T-Rex.
reference: They have feet with scales and claws, they lay eggs, and they walk on their two back legs like a T-Rex.

source: Klostu olgeta komputa we ol i yusim long tude em ol i save senisim infomesen i stap long 'code' bilong ol 'binary number'.
reference: Virtually all computers in use today are based on the manipulation of information which is coded in the form of binary numbers.

source: Wanpela 'binary number' em i ken i gat tupela velu (value), dispela em i 0 o 1, na ol dispela namba em ol i save kolim olsem ol 'binary digit' - o 'bits' long toktok bilong komputa.
reference: A binary number can have only one of two values, i.e. 0 or 1, and these numbers are referred to as binary digits - or bits, to use computer jargon.

source: 'Internal poisoning' em i no inap long kamap ples klia hariap. Ol sain, kain olsem traut em ol bikpela sain taim ol lain i no inap long mekim wanpela 'diagnosis' hariap.
reference: Internal poisoning may not be immediately apparent. Symptoms, such as vomiting are sufficiently general that an immediate diagnosis cannot be made.

source: Ol nambawan samting we i soim olsem 'internal poisoning' i kamap em wanpela kontena bilong marasin o 'toxic chemical' bilong haus i op na i stap.
reference: The best indication of internal poisoning may be the presence of an open container of medication or toxic household chemicals.

source: Sekim 'label' long ol 'first aid instruction' bilong dipela posin.
reference: Check the label for specific first aid instructions for that specific poison.

source: Dispela hap tok 'bug' em ol entomolojist (entomologist) i save yusim long toktok long dispela grup bilong ol binatang.
reference: The term bug is used by entomologists in a formal sense for this group of insects.

source: Dispela hap tok i kisim mining bilong em long Bed-bugs, ol dispela binatang we i save parasait long ol manmeri.
reference: This term derives from ancient familiarity with Bed-bugs, which are insects highly adapted to parasitize humans.

source: Ol Assasin-bugs na ol Bed-bugs em ol i 'nidiocolous', ol i save stap long ples we ol i bon long en o long samting we i save givim ol kaikai.
reference: Both Assassin-bugs and Bed-bugs are nidicolous, adapted to living in nest or housing of their host.

source: Long kainkain hap bilong United States of America, i gat kain olsem 400,000 'case' bilong Multiple Sclerosis (MS), mekim em i kamap olsem nambawan 'neurological disease' insait long ol bikpela manmeri.
reference: Across the United States of America, there are approximately 400,000 known cases of Multiple Sclerosis (MS), leaving it as the leading neurological disease in younger and middle aged adults.

source: MS em i wanpela sik we i save kamap long 'central nervous system', dispela sistem em brein (brain), 'spinal cord' na 'optic nerve' i kamapim.
reference: MS is a disease that affects the central nervous system, which is made up of the brain, the spinal cord and the optic nerve.

source: Risets i bin painimaut olsem sans bilong ol meri long kisim MS em i tupela taim moa long ol man.
reference: Research has found that females are two times more likely to have MS then males.

source: Wanpela marit i ken mekim disisen olsem em i no gutpela long ol, ol em i no gutpela long pikinini bilong ol, sapos ol i gat wanpela bebi.
reference: A couple may decide it is not in their best interest, or in the interest of their child, to raise a baby.

source: Ol dispela kain marit i ken mekim disisen long putim bebi bilong ol i go long 'adoption'.
reference: These couples may choose to make an adoption plan for their baby.

source: Insait long wanpela 'adoption', ol papamama we i kamapim dispela pikinini bai stopim 'right' bilong olsem ol papamama na narapela marit i ken lukautim dispela pikinini.
reference: In an adoption, the birth parents terminate their parental rights so that another couple may parent the child.

source: As tingting bilong sains (science) em long painimaut wanem we dispela graun i wok taim ol yusim ol 'scientific method'. Dispela metod (method) i save stiaim planti bilong ol 'scientific research'.
reference: Science’s main goal is to figure out the way the world works through the scientific method. This method in fact guides most scientific research.

source: Em i no i stap em yet, 'experimentation', na wanpela 'experiment' em i wanpela 'test' ol save yusim long rausim wanpela o moa bilong ol tingting we ol i bin kamapim, pasin bilong askim ol kwesten, na mekim stadi tu i save givim stia long 'scientific research'.
reference: It isn’t alone though, experimentation, and an experiment is a test that is used to eliminate one or more of the possible hypotheses, asking questions, and making observations also guide scientific research.

source: Ol lain i save stadi long 'nature' na ol 'philosophy' i save lukluk long ol 'classical texts', kain olsem, Bible long tok ples Latin.
reference: Naturalists and philosophers focused on classical texts and, in particular, on the Bible in Latin.

source: Ol samting we ol i tok orait long en em ol tingting bilong Aristotle long sains (science), wantaim saikoloji (psychology).
reference: Accepted were Aristotle's views on all matters of science, including psychology.

source: Taim save bilong ol Greek i go daun, West i lusim ol tingting bilong Greek long sains (science) na filosofi (philosophy).
reference: As knowledge of Greek declined, the West found itself cut off from its Greek philosophical and scientific roots.

source: Planti savemanmeri i lukim olsem ol 'rhythm' we i kamap long bodi na pasin bilong ol animal na manmeri i save bihainim ol 'cycle' bilong ol samting we i stap autsait long bodi na ol 'biological clock' bilong bodi.
reference: Many observed rhythms in physiology and behavior often crucially depend on the presence of endogenous cycles and their production through biological clocks.

source: Ol 'periodic rhythms', em ol i no kamap bikos long ol samting we i stap autsait tasol, dispela em ol lain i bin stadi na lukim olsem em i kamap long planti ol samting we i gat laip, wantaim ol 'bacteria', 'fungi', plent (plant) na animal.
reference: Periodic rhythms, which are not simply responses to external periodic cues, have been documented for most living beings, including bacteria, fungi, plants, and animals.

source: Ol 'biological clock' em ol samting we i save mentenim ol yet na i save kamap ol 'electric currents' na ol i save kontinu (continue) long ron maski ol samting long autsait we i save mekim ol i ron i no stap.
reference: Biological clocks are self sustaining oscillators which will continue a period of free-running cycling even in the absence of external cues.

source: 'Experiment' bilong Hershey and Chase em i wanpela bilong ol samting we i tok olsem DNA em i wanpela 'genetic material'.
reference: The Hershey and Chase experiment was one of the leading suggestions that DNA was a genetic material.

source: Hershey na Chase i bin yusim 'phages', o ol vaires (virus), long putim DNA bilong ol yet i go insait long wanpela 'bacterium'.
reference: Hershey and Chase used phages, or viruses, to implant their own DNA into a bacterium.

source: Ol i mekim tupela 'experiment' we tupela i makim DNA insait long 'phage' wantaim wanpela 'radioactive phosphorus' o tupela i makim 'protein' bilong 'phage' wantaim 'radioactive sulfur'.
reference: They did two experiments marking either the DNA in the phage with a radioactive phosphorus or the protein of the phage with radioactive sulfur.

source: Ol 'mutation' i ken kamapim kainkain samting na ol i save kamap bikos long wanem kain 'mutation', impoten wok bilong dispela hap 'genetic material' na sapos dispela ol 'cell' em ol 'germ-line cell'.
reference: Mutations can have a variety of different effects depending on the type of mutation, the significance of the piece of genetic material affected and whether the cells affected are germ-line cells.

source: Ol 'mutation' we i kamap insait long ol 'germ-line cell' tasol em ol i ken i go long ol pikinini, tasol ol 'mutation' we i kamap long narapela hap i ken kamapim 'cell-death' o kensa.
reference: Only mutations in germ-line cells can be passed on to children, while mutations elsewhere can cause cell-death or cancer.

source: 'Nature-based tourism' em i save pulim ol manmeri we i intres long lukim ol eria bilong 'nature' na amamas long lukim ol ples, wantaim ol plent (plent) na animal bilong dispela eria.
reference: Nature-based tourism attracts people interested in visiting natural areas for the purpose of enjoying the scenery, including plant and animal wildlife.

source: Eksampel bilong ol 'on-site activity' em painim pis, kisim foto, lukim ol pisin, na raun long ol 'park' na stadim infomesen long 'ecosystem'.
reference: Examples of on-site activities include hunting, fishing, photography, bird watching, and visiting parks and studying information about the ecosystem.

source: Wanpela eksampel em raun, kisim foto, na kisim save long ol 'orangatuang' taim yu go long Borneo.
reference: An example is visiting, photographing, and learning about organgatuangs in Borneo.

source: Olgeta moning. ol manmeri i save lusim liklik kantri taun taim ol i kalap long kar long go long ol ples bilong ol long wok na ol i save abrus ol narapela lain we ples bilong ol long wok i stap long ples we ol i lusim nau tasol na i kam.
reference: Every morning, people leave small country towns in cars to go their workplace and are passed by others whose work destination is the place they have just left.

source: Insait long dispela 'transport shuttle' olgeta lain i konek wantaim, na ol i sapotim wanpela 'transport system' we ol 'private car' i kamapim.
reference: In this dynamic transport shuttle everyone is somehow connected with, and supporting, a transport system based on private cars.

source: Nau sains (science) i soim olsem dispela traipela 'carbon economy' em i rausim 'biosphere' long wanpela 'stable state' we i bin sapotim ol manmeri long tupela milien yia i go pinis.
reference: Science now indicates that this massive carbon economy has dislodged the biosphere from one of its stable states that has supported human evolution for the past two million years.

source: Olgeta manmeri i save stap insait long sosaiti (society) na yusim 'transportation system'. Klostu olgeta lain i save komplen long ol 'transportation system'.
reference: Everyone participates in society and uses transportation systems. Almost everyone complains about transportation systems.

source: Long ol 'developed country' yu bai no inap long harim wankain mak bilong komplen long kwaliti bilong wara ol bris i pundaun.
reference: In developed countries you seldom hear similar levels of complaints about water quality or bridges falling down.

source: Bilong wanem na ol 'transportation system' i save kamapim ol dispela kain komplen, bilong wanem na ol i no save wok gut long olgeta de? Ol 'transportation engineer' i no save long mekim wok bilong ol? O, i gat samting we i bikpela moa i wok long kamap?
reference: Why do transportation systems engender such complaints, why do they fail on a daily basis? Are transportation engineers just incompetent? Or is something more fundamental going on?

source: 'Traffic Flow' em i stadi bilong muvmen (movement) bilong wanwan draiva na kara namel long tupela poin na wanem kain samting ol i save mekim long wanpela narapela.
reference: Traffic Flow is the study of the movement of individual drivers and vehicles between two points and the interactions they make with one another.

source: Tasol, stadi bilong 'traffic flow' em i hat bikos pasin bilong draiva em wanpela i no inap long tok wanem samting bai i kamap long mak we wan handret pesen (percent) korek.
reference: Unfortunately, studying traffic flow is difficult because driver behavior cannot be predicted with one-hundred percent certainty.

source: Tasol, ol draiva i save mekim pasin long mak we i 'consistent', olsem na, ol 'traffic' i save i gat sampela samting i 'consistent' long ol na dispela em ol savemanmeri i ken soim wantaim ol namba bilong 'mathematics'.
reference: Fortunately, drivers tend to behave within a reasonably consistent range; thus, traffic streams tend to have some reasonable consistency and can be roughly represented mathematically.

source: Long mekim 'traffic flow' i kamap gutpela moa, ol rilesensip i mas kamap namel long tripela bikpela samting: (1) 'flow', (2) 'density', and (3) 'velocity'.
reference: To better represent traffic flow, relationships have been established between the three main characteristics: (1) flow, (2) density, and (3) velocity.

source: Ol dispela rilesensip i save helpim long mekim 'planning', 'design', na 'operation' bilong ol 'roadway facilities'.
reference: These relationships help in planning, design, and operations of roadway facilities.

source: Ol insek em ol namba wan animal long stap long win. Dispela pasin bilong ol long flai i helpim ol long abrusim ol birua bilong ol na painim kaikai na pren bilong ol isi tru.
reference: Insects were the first animals to take to the air. Their ability to fly helped them evade enemies more easily and find food and mates more efficiently.

source: Planti ol insek i ken foldim wing bilong ol i kam pas wantaim bodi bilong ol na dispela em i gutpela samting.
reference: Most insects have the advantage of being able to fold their wings back along the body.

source: Dispela i mekim na ol i gat planti liklik ples bilong hait long ol animal we i laik kaikaim ol.
reference: This gives them a wider range of small places to hide from predators.

source: Tude, ol insek (insect) we i no save foldim wing bilong ol em ol 'dragon flies' na ol 'mayflies'.
reference: Today, the only insects that cannot fold back their wings are dragon flies and mayflies.

source: Sampela tausen yia i go pinis, wanpela man nem bilong em Aristarchus i tok olsem Solar System i save muv raunim Sun.
reference: Thousands of years ago, a man called Aristarchus said that the Solar System moved around the Sun.

source: Ol sampela manmeri i ting olsem em i tok stret, tasol planti manmeri i bilim long oposit (opposite); ol i bilip olsem Solar System i save muv raunim Earth, wantaim Sun (na ol narapela sta tu).
reference: Some people thought he was right but many people believed the opposite; that the Solar System moved around the Earth, including the Sun (and even the other stars).

source: Dispela em i luk olsem i stret, bikos yumi no save pilim olsem Earth i muv, a?
reference: This seems sensible, because the Earth doesn't feel as if it's moving, does it?

source: Amazon River em i namba tu longpela wara na namba wan bikpela wara long Earth. Em i karim wara we mak bilong em i 8-pela taim moa long namba tu bikpela wara.
reference: The Amazon River is the second longest and the biggest river on Earth. It carries more than 8 times as much water as the second biggest river.

source: Na tu, Amazon em namba wan wara we namel bilong em i bikpela tru long Earth, na mak bilong namel bilong en em i sikspela 'mile'.
reference: The Amazon is also the widest river on Earth, at times six miles wide.

source: 20 pesen (percent) bilong wara we i save kamaut long ol wara bilong plenet (planet) i save kam long Amazon.
reference: A full 20 percent of the water that pours out of the planet's rivers into the oceans comes from the Amazon.

source: Bikpela Amazon River em i 6,387 km (3,980 miles). Em i save kolektim ol wara i kam long planti tausen liklik wara.
reference: The main Amazon River is 6,387 km (3,980 miles). It collects water from thousands of smaller rivers.

source: Maski ol pasin bilong mekim 'pyramid-bilding' long ston em i kontinu (continue) i go inap long pinis bilong Old Kingdom, ol i no winim sais na save bilong ol lain i wokim ol 'pyramid' bilong Giza.
reference: Although pyramid-building in stone continued until the end of the Old Kingdom, the pyramids of Giza were never surpassed in their size and the technical excellence of their construction.

source: Ol lain Egypt bilong New Kingdom i tingting olsem ol dispela bilding bilong ol tumbuna bilong ol em i narakain tru, long dispela taim ol dispela 'pyramid' i gat moa long tausen krismas.
reference: New Kingdom ancient Egyptians marvelled at their predecessors monuments, which were then well over a thousand year old.

source: Populesen (population) bilong Vatican City em i kain olsem 800. Dispela em nambawan liklik kantri tru insait long wol na kantri we i gat liklik populesen tru.
reference: Vatican City's population is around 800. It is the smallest independent country in the world and the country with the lowest population.

source: Vatican City i save yusim tok ples Italian insait long ol lo bilong em na long mekim ol 'official communication'.
reference: Vatican City uses Italian in its legislation and official communications.

source: Tok ples Italian em ol lain i save wok insait long steit (state) i save yusim tu long toktok long olgeta de na ol i save yusim Latin long mekim ol seremoni (ceremony) bilong lotu.
reference: Italian is also the everyday language used by most of those who work in the state while Latin is often used in religious ceremonies.

source: Olgeta sitisen (citizen) bilong Vatican City em ol i Roman Catholic.
reference: All citizens of Vatican City are Roman Catholic.

source: Ol manmeri i bin save long ol bikpela kemikel elemen (chemical element) kain olsem gol, silva, na kopa (copper) long bipo taim, bikos ol i bin painim ol insait long envairomen long 'native form' na em i isi long painim ol wantaim ol tul (tool) bilong bipo.
reference: People have known about basic chemical elements such as gold, silver, and copper from antiquity, as these can all be discovered in nature in native form and are relatively simple to mine with primitive tools.

source: Aristotle, wanpela filosofa (philosopher), i kamapim tingting olsem olgeta samting i kamap long ol wanpela o moa bilong ol foapela elemen (elements) i miks wantaim. Ol dispela elemen em graun, wara, win, na paia.
reference: Aristotle, a philosopher, theorised that everything is made up of a mixture of one or more of four elements. They were earth, water, air, and fire.

source: Dispela em i wankain olsem foapela 'states of matter' (na i bihainim wankain oda): strong, wara, ges (gas), na 'plasma', em i tok tu olsem ol i senis na kamapim nupela samting long mekim ol samting we yumi i lukim.
reference: This was more like the four states of matter (in the same order): solid, liquid, gas, and plasma, though he also theorised that they change into new substances to form what we see.

source: Ol 'alloy'em ol samting we i gat tupela o moa metel (metal) insait long en. No ken lus tingting olsem i gat planti elemen (element) insait long 'periodic table'.
reference: Alloys are basically a mixture of two or more metals. Don't forget that there are many elements on the periodic table.

source: Ol elemen (element) kain olsem 'calcium' na 'potassium' em ol i save lukim ol olsem ol metel (metal). I gat ol narapela metel tu olsem silva na gol.
reference: Elements like calcium and potassium are considered metals. Of course, there are also metals like silver and gold.

source: Yu ken i gat ol 'alloy' we i gat ol liklik mak bilong ol elemen (element) we i no metel (metal) kain olsem 'carbon' insait long ol.
reference: You can also have alloys that include small amounts of non-metallic elements like carbon.

source: Olgeta samting insait long Universe em ol i kamap long 'matter'. Olgeta 'matter' i gat ol liklik samting insait long ol we ol i kolim 'atom'.
reference: Everything in the Universe is made of matter. All matter is made of tiny particles called atoms.

source: Ol 'atom' em ol i liklik liklik tru na trilien (trillion) bilong ol bai i inap long fit i go inap long wanpela 'full stop' long pinis bilong wanpela sentens (sentence).
reference: Atoms are so incredibly tiny that trillions of them could fit into the period at the end of this sentence.

source: Olsem na, pensil (pencil) em i bin wanpela gutpela pren bilong planti manmeri taim em i bin kamap.
reference: Thus, the pencil was a good friend to many people when it came out.

source: Tasol, taim ol nupela rot bilong rait i kamap, pensil (pencil) i kisim wanpela posisen we i daunbilo na i no gat planti i yusim em.
reference: Sadly, as newer methods of writing have emerged, the pencil has been relegated to lesser status and uses.

source: Nau ol manmeri i save raitim ol tok save long ol komputa skrin (computer screen), na ol i no kam klostu long wanpela sapena (sharpener).
reference: People now write messages on computer screens, never having to come close to a sharpener.

source: Yumi ken tingting long wanem samting bai i kamap long ol kibod (keyboard) taim wanpela nupela samting i kamap.
reference: One can only wonder what the keyboard will become when something newer comes along.

source: 'Fission bomb' em i save wok bihainim lo we i save kisim eneji na kamapim wanpela 'nucleus' wantaim planti 'protons' na 'neutrons'.
reference: The fission bomb works on the principle that it takes energy to put together a nucleus with many protons and neutrons.

source: Wankain liklik olsem yu wok long pusim wanpela hevipela samting i go antap long wanpela liklik maunten. Pasin bilong 'splitting nucleus' gen i save lusim sampela bilong ol dispela eneji.
reference: Sort of like rolling a heavy cart up a hill. Splitting the nucleus up again then releases some of that energy.

source: Sampela 'atom' i gat 'unstable nuclei' na dispela em i min olsem ol i save bruk taim yu tasim liklik o i no tasim.
reference: Some atoms have unstable nuclei which means that they tend to break apart with little or no nudging.

source: Antap bilong Moon em i gat ol ston na das (dust). 'Outer layer' bilong Moon em ol i kolim 'crust'.
reference: The surface of the Moon is made of rocks and dust. The outer layer of the Moon is called the crust.

source: 'Crust' bilong em i kain olsem 70 km 'thick' long sait i stap klostu na 100 km 'thick' long sait i stap longwe.
reference: The crust is about 70 km thick on the near side and 100 km thick on the far side.

source: Em i 'thin' moa aninit long 'maria' na 'thick' moa aninit long 'highlands'.
reference: It is thinner under the maria and thicker under the highlands.

source: I luk olsem bai i gat moa 'maria' long sait i stap klostu bikos 'crust' em i 'thin' moa. Em i isi moa long 'lava' long kam antap long autsait.
reference: There may be more maria on the near side because the crust is thinner. It was easier for lava to rise up to the surface.

source: Ol 'content theories' i save lukluk long painimaut wanem ol samting i save kirapim bel bilong ol manmeri o i tasim bel na tingting bilong ol.
reference: Content theories are centered on finding what makes people tick or appeals to them.

source: Ol dispela 'theory' i tok olsem ol manmeri i gat sampela samting ol i nidim na laikim we i stap insait long ol taim ol i kamap bikpela.
reference: These theories suggest that people have certain needs and/or desires which have been internalized as they mature to adulthood.

source: Ol dispela 'theory' i save lukluk long wanem samting insait long sampela manmeri i save mekim ol i laikim ol samting we ol i mekim na wanem ol samting insait long envairomen (environment) bai wokim na ol i mekim wanpela samting o i no mekim wanpela samting.
reference: These theories look at what it is about certain people that make them want the things that they do and what things in their environment will make them do or not do certain things.

source: Tupela 'content theory' we planti lain i save long en em Hierarchy of Needs Theory na Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.
reference: Two popular content theories are Maslow's Hierarchy of Needs Theory and Hertzberg's Two Factor Theory.

source: Sapos yumi skelim olgeta samting, tupela pasin i kamap ples klia taim ol meneja i stat long go pas long ol wanlain bilong ol. Long wanpela sait em ol i laik stap olsem “wanpela bilong ol mangi long grup” (meri long grup).
reference: Generally speaking, two behaviors can emerge as managers begin to lead their former peers. One end of the spectrum is trying to remain “one of the guys” (or gals).

source: Dispela kain meneja em i save painim hat long mekim ol disisen we ol lain i no laikim, givim mekim save long ol lain i brukim lo, sekim wok pefomens (performance) bilong ol wokmanmeri, givim wok long ol wokmanmeri, na tokaut stret long ol wokmanmeri olsem ol i mekim rong.
reference: This type of manager has difficulty making unpopular decisions, performing disciplinary action, performance evaluations, assigning responsibility, and holding people accountable.

source: Long narapela sait, wanpela meneja i senis i go na kamap olsem wanpela man o meri we ol lain i bo save long em na em i pilim olsem em i mas senisim olgeta samting we tim i wok long mekim na mekim olgeta samting long strong bilong ol yet.
reference: At the other end of the spectrum, one morphs into an unrecognizable individual that feels he or she must change everything the team has been doing and make it their own.

source: Bihain long olgeta samting, em i wok bilong lida long mekim tim i mekim gut wok o i no mekim gut wok.
reference: After all, the leader is ultimately responsible for the success and failure of the team.

source: Dispela pasin i save mekim hevi i kamap namel long ol lida na ol tim.
reference: This behavior oftentimes results in rifts between the leaders and the rest of the team.

source: Ol tim we i save wok wantaim long komputa i ken i gat ol wankain stendet bilong mekim gut wok wankain olsem ol tim we i save wok wantaim long ofis, tasol i gat sampela liklik samting i narakain.
reference: Virtual teams are held to the same standards of excellence as conventional teams, but there are subtle differences.

source: Ol tim we i save wok wantaim long komputa i save wok stap olsem man o meri we i save bungim ol grup we i wok wantaim long ofis.
reference: Virtual team members often function as the point of contact for their immediate physical group.

source: Ol i ken mekim samting long laik bilong ol moa long ol tim we i save wok insait long ofis taim ol bung wantaim ol narapela tim memba long taim we i gutpela long ol kainkain 'time zone' na dispela em ol lokel meneja i no inap long klia long en.
reference: They often have more autonomy than conventional team members as their teams may meet according to varying time zones which may not be understood by their local management.

source: Wanpela trupela “'invisible team'” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) em i wanpela narakain samting i stap insait long ol tim we i save wok wantaim long komputa.
reference: The presence of a true “invisible team” (Larson and LaFasto, 1989, p109) is also a unique component of a virtual team.

source: Dispela “'invisible team'” em i wanpela 'management team' we wanwan memba bilong tim i save ripot i go long en. Dispela 'invisible team' i save makim stendet bilong wanwan memba long bihainim.
reference: The “invisible team” is the management team to which each of the members report. The invisible team sets the standards for each member.

source: Wai na wanpela oganaisesen bai laik long mekim wok we i save kisim planti taim? Dispela em wok bilong kamapim wanpela oganaisesen we i save lainim samting. Wanpela bilong ol as tingting bilong mekim 'organizational learning' i kamap insait long oganaisesen em pasin bilong 'innovation'.
reference: Why would an organization want to go through the time consuming process of establishing a learning organization? One goal for putting organizational learning concepts into practice is innovation.

source: Taim ol dipatmen bilong wanpela oganaisesen i yusim gut olgeta risos, 'creativity' na 'ingenuity' i ken kamap.
reference: When all available resources are effectively used across the functional departments of an organization, creativity and ingenuity can transpire.

source: Bikos long dispela, proses (process) bilong wanpela oganaisesen long wok wantaim na daunim wanpela hatpela samting i ken kamap ol nupela narapela kain proses bilong inapim nid bilong kastoma.
reference: As a result, the process of an organization working together to overcome an obstacle can lead to a new innovative process to serve the customer's need.

source: Bipo long wanpela oganaisesen i ken i gat ol nupela na narapela kain we bilong mekim samting, ol lida i mas kamapim wanpela kalsa bilong kamapim ol nupela na narapela kain samting na tu, i mas i gat pasin bilong serim save na lainim samting wantaim.
reference: Before an organization can be innovative, leadership must create a culture of innovation as well as shared knowledge and organizational learning.

source: Angel (2006), i tok klia long Continuum approach olsem wanpela metod (method) we ol i yusim long helpim oganaisesen i kamap long mak we i antap moa long sait bilong pefomen (performance).
reference: Angel (2006), explains the Continuum approach as a method being used to help organizations reach a higher level of performance.

source: Neurobiological infomesen i givim evidens (evidence) long wanpela 'theoretical approach' long investigesen bilong 'cognition'. Olsem na em i mekim eria bilong risets i kamap liklik moa na mekim em i kamap long 'exact'.
reference: Neurobiological data provide physical evidence for a theoretical approach to the investigation of cognition. Therefore it narrows the research area and makes it much more exact.

source: Rilesensip namel long 'brain pathology' na pasin i sapotim ol saintis insait long risets bilong ol.
reference: The correlation between brain pathology and behaviour supports scientists in their research.

source: Ol i save long longpela taim pinis olsem ol kainkain bagarap i kamap long brein (brain), ol 'trauma', ol 'lesion', na ol 'tumour' i ken mekim pasin i senis na kamapim senis long tingting.
reference: It has been known for a long time that different types of brain damage, traumas, lesions, and tumours affect behaviour and cause changes in some mental functions.

source: Dispela ol nupela teknoloji we i kamap i helpim mipela long lukim na mekim investigesen long ol straksa (structure) na proses (process) bilong brein (brain) we mipela i no bin lukim bipo.
reference: The rise of new technologies allows us to see and investigate brain structures and processes never seen before.

source: Dispela i save givim mipela nupela infomesen na meteriel long kamapim ol 'simulation model' we i ken helpim yumi long klia gut long ol proses (process) we i save kamap insait long tingting bilong mipela.
reference: This provides us with a lot of information and material to build simulation models which help us to understand processes in our mind.

source: Maski AI em i mekim ol lain i tingim 'science fiction', AI em i kamapim wanpela impoten eria insait long 'computer science', em i save mekim wok long sait bilong pasin, lainim samting, mekim senis insait long wanpela masin.
reference: Although AI has a strong connotation of science fiction, AI forms a very important branch of computer science, dealing with behavior, learning and intelligent adaptation in a machine.

source: Risets insait long AI em i wok bilong mekim masin i mekim ol wok we i save kamap olgeta taim na i nidim save bilong wanpela wok long mekim disisen.
reference: Research in AI involves making machines to automate tasks that require intelligent behavior.

source: Eksampel em kontrolim samting, plenim na makim taim bilong samting, save bilong ansarim ol kwesten bilong kastoma, na luksave long 'handwriting', vois na pes.
reference: Examples include control, planning and scheduling, the ability to answer customer diagnoses and questions, as well as handwriting recognition, voice and face.

source: Dispela kain ol samting i gat wanwan eria bilong stadi bilong ol yet, na i lukluk long ol rot bilong givim ol ansa long ol problem we i save kamap long olgeta de.
reference: Such things have become separate disciplines, which focus on providing solutions to real life problems.

source: Nau ol i save yusim dispela AI sistem insait long ol eria bilong 'economics', 'medecine', 'engineering' na 'military', na ol i bin yusim em long bildim sampela komputa bilong haus na 'video game software applications'.
reference: The AI ​​system is now often used in the fields of economics, medicine, engineering and the military, as has been built in several home computer and video game software applications.

source: Ol 'field trip' em ol i impoten pat bilong ol klasrum. Planti taim tisa i save laik long kisim ol sumatin bilong em i go long ol ples we ol i no inap long kalap long bas (bus) na i go.
reference: Field trips are a large part of any classroom. Quite often a teacher would love to take her students places to which a bus trip is not an option.

source: Teknoloji (technology) i givim ansa wantaim 'field trip' we i kamap long komputa. Ol sumating i ken lukluk long ol 'museum artifact', lukim wanpela 'aquarium', ol lukluk long wanpela naispela 'art' taim ol i sindaun insait long klas.
reference: Technology offers the solution with virtual field trips. Students can look at museum artifacts, visit an aquarium, or admire beautiful art while sitting with their class.

source: Pasin bilong serim wanpela 'field trip' long komputa em i wanpela gutpela we tu long tingting lon wanpela raun na serim ol samting i bin kamap wantaim klas bilong bihaintaim.
reference: Sharing a field trip virtually is also a great way to reflect a on a trip and share experiences with future classes.

source: Eksampel, long wanwan yia ol sumatin long Bennet School long North Carolina i save disainim wanpela websait long raun bilong ol i go long State Capital, na long wanwan yia dispela websait i kisim nupela stail, tasol ol olpela 'version' i save stap yet 'online' olsem wanpela 'scrapebook'.
reference: For example, each year students from Bennet School in North Carolina design a website about their trip to the State Capital, each year the website gets remodeled, but old versions are kept online to serve as a scrapbook.

source: Ol blog i ken kisim helpim sumatin long rait gut. Planti taim ol sumatin i statim blog bilong ol wantaim 'grammar' na 'spelling' we i no gutpela, na taim i gat lain bai i ritim ol bai i senis na i rait gut moa.
reference: Blogs can also help improve student writing. While students often begin their blog experience with sloppy grammar and spelling, the presence of an audience generally changes that.

source: Bikos ol sumatin em ol i save mekim planti toktok sapos samting i no stret, dispela sumatin we i raitim blog bai i stat long rait gut long abrusim ol toktok we i tok olsem em no rait gut.
reference: Since students are often the most critical audience, the blog writer begins to strive to improve writing to avoid criticism.

source: Na tu, 'blogging' "i save fosim ol sumatin long save moa long ol samting we i wok long kamap long komuniti we ol i stap long en." Dispela nid long inapim nid bilong ol lain i ritim blog bilong ol i mekim na ol sumatin i gat moa save na kamap 'interesting' (Toto, 2004).
reference: Also blogging "forces students to become more savvy about the world around them." The need to feed the interest of the audience inspires students to be clever and interesting (Toto, 2004).

source: 'Blogging' em i wanpela tul (tool) we i save mekim ol lain i wok wantaim, na i strongim ol sumating long surikim samting ol i lainim i go autsait moa long wanem samting ol i save lainim long wanpela de insait long skul.
reference: Blogging is a tool that inspires collaboration, and encourages students to extend learning well beyond the traditional school day.

source: Yusim ol blog long gutpela na stretpela we "i ken mekim ol sumatin i ken skelim ol samting na save long wanem samting i no stret na wanem we bilong stret; taim ol wok wantaim ol meteriel long Internet, ol sumatin i ken save long wanem mak ol i stap long en taim ol skelim samting we ol narapela i raitim na tu ol i ken givim tingting bilong ol long ol samting we i wok long kamap (Oravec, 2002).
reference: Appropriate use of blogs "can empower students to become more analytical and critical; through actively responding to Internet materials, students can define their positions in the context of others' writings as well as outline their own perspectives on particular issues (Oravec, 2002).

source: Ottawa em i kapitol bilong Canada we ol lain i save yusim tupela tok ples na em i gat ol 'art gallery' na 'museum' we i soim taim bipo na taim bilong tude long Canada.
reference: Ottawa is Canada's charming, bilingual capital and features an array of art galleries and museums that showcase Canada's past and present.

source: Longwe long saut em Niagara Fall na long not em Musoka, wanplea ples we i luk nais na i gat ol narapela ples moa i stap.
reference: Farther south is Niagara Falls and the north is home to the untapped natural beauty of the Muskoka and beyond.

source: Ol lain long autsait i tok olsem olgeta dispela samting na sampela moa i soim Ontario olsem em i ples we i soim tru tru kala bilong Canada.
reference: All these things and more highlight Ontario as what is considered quintessentially Canadian by outsiders.

source: Ol bikpela eria longwe long not em i no gat planti manmeri na sampela ples we i no gat manmeri long en.
reference: Large areas further north are quite sparsely populated and some is nearly uninhabited wilderness.

source: Wanpela samting bilong skelim insait long populesen we i mekim planti lain i kirap nogut: I gat moa African American i stap long US winim namba bilong olgeta sitisen (citizen) bilong Canada.
reference: For a comparison of population that surprises many: There are more African Americans living in the US than there are Canadian citizens.

source: Eastern African Islanders em ol i stap long Indian Ocean autsait long 'eastern coast' bilong Africa.
reference: The East African Islands are in the Indian Ocean off the eastern coast of Africa.

source: Madagascar em i wanpela bikpela tru, na em i kain olsem wanpela kontinen (continent) bilong em, taim yumi lukluk long sait bilong 'wildlife'.
reference: Madagascar is by far the biggest, and a continent on its own when it comes to wildlife.

source: Planti bilong ol liklik ailan em ol i stap olsem ol indipenden (independent) kantri, o ol i save stap olsem hap bilong France, na ol i save stap olsem ol 'luxury beach resort'.
reference: Most of the smaller islands are independent nations, or associated with France, and known as luxury beach resorts.

source: Na tu, ol Arab i bin kisim Islam i kam long ol dispela hap, na ol i kamap olsem bikpela lotu insait long Comoros na Mayotte.
reference: The Arabs also brought Islam to the lands, and it took in a big way in the Comoros and Mayotte.

source: Pasin na tingting bilong ol lain long Europe na 'colonialism' i stat long '15th century', taim eksplora (explorer) bilong Portugal Vasco de Gama i painim Cape Route taim em i stap long Europe na i go long India.
reference: European influence and colonialism began in the 15th century, as Portuguese explorer Vasco da Gama found the Cape Route from Europe to India.

source: Long hap not bilong rijen em Sahel i raunim, na long hap na hap wes Atlantic Ocean i raunim.
reference: In the north the region is bounded by the Sahel, and in the south and west by the Atlantic Ocean.

source: Ol meri: Em i gutpela olsem ol meri i we i go long hap i mas tok olsem ol i marit, maski ol i no marit tru tru.
reference: Women: It is recommended that any women travellers say that they are married, regardless of actual marital status.

source: Em i gutpela long werim wanpela ring (no ken werim ring we i luk ekspensif).
reference: It is helpful to also wear a ring (just not one that looks too expensive.

source: Ol meri i mas luksave olsem pasin bilong narapela kalsa i ken mekim na samting we ol i ting olsem haresmen (harassment) i ken kamap na planti taim ol lain ken bihaim yu, holim han bilong yu na kain olsem.
reference: Women should realize that cultural differences may result in what they would consider harassment and it is not uncommon to be followed, grabbed by the arm, etc.

source: Yu mas strong na tok nogat long ol man, no ken pret long sanap strong na toktok (pasin em i pasin bilong dispela ples, em i no orait long ol i mekim olsem!).
reference: Be firm in turning down men, and don't be afraid to stand your ground (cultural differences or not, it doesn't make it ok!).

source: Siti bilong Casablanca long tude em ol man Berber we i save painim pis i kamapim long '10th century' BCE, na ol Phoenicians, Romans, na Merenid i bin yusim olsem wanpela impoten port we ol i kolim Anfa.
reference: The modern city of Casablanca was founded by Berber fishermen in the 10th century BCE, and was used by the Phoenicians, Romans, and the Merenids as a strategic port called Anfa.

source: Ol Portuguese i bin bagarapim em na bildim em gen aninit long nem Casa Branca, tasol ol i lusim dispela ples bihain long wanpela guria i kamap long 1755.
reference: The Portuguese destroyed it and rebuilt it under the name Casa Branca, only to abandon it after an earthquake in 1755.

source: Ol 'sultan' bilong Morocco i bildim dispela siti gen olsem Daru l-Badya na ol treida (trader) bilong Spain i kolim em olsem Casablanca taim ol i mekim ol senta bilong ol long hap.
reference: The Moroccan sultan rebuilt the city as Daru l-Badya and it was given the name Casablanca by Spanish traders who established trading bases there.

source: Insait long olgeta hap bilong Casablanca em i wanpela bilong ol ples we i no gat gutpela hap bilong baim ol samting.
reference: Casablanca is one of the least interesting places to shop in all of Morocco.

source: Long olpela Medina em i isi long painim ol ples we i save salim ol samting bilong ol Morocco, kain olsem 'tagine', 'pottery', 'leather goods', 'hookahs', na ol kainkain 'geegaws', tasol olgeta dispela samting em i bilong ol turis long baim.
reference: Around the old Medina it's easy to find places selling traditional Moroccan goods, such as tagines, pottery, leather goods, hookahs, and a whole spectrum of geegaws, but it's all for the tourists.

source: Goma em i wanpela siti we ol turis i save laik i go long en insait long Democratic Republic of Congo long hap ist klostu long kantri Rwanda.
reference: Goma is a tourist city of the Democratic Republic of Congo in the extreme east near Rwanda.

source: Long yia 2002, hotpela paia bilong volkeno Nyiragongo i bin bagarapim Goma na karamapim planti bilong ol rot bilong dispela taun, dispela i kamap bikpela tru long namel bilong taun.
reference: In 2002 Goma was destroyed by lava from the Nyiragongo volcano which buried most of the town’s streets, particularly the town centre.

source: Maski Goma em seif liklik, ol turis we i go lukim ol ples autsait long Goma i mas painimaut na save gut long wanem kain pait i wok long kamap long North Kivu provins.
reference: While Goma is reasonably safe, any visits outside of Goma should be researched to understand the state of the fighting that persists in the North Kivu province.

source: Dispela siti em i ples bilong i stap long en na klaimim Nyiragongo volkeno wantaim ol lain bilong painim Moutain Gorilla. Prais bilong ol dispela lain bilong painim gorila i orait moa long ol narapela insait long Africa.
reference: The city is also the base to climb the Nyiragongo volcano along with some of the cheapest Mountain Gorilla tracking in Africa.

source: Yu ken yusim boda-boda (motobaik teksi) long raun insait long Goma. Lokel prais bilong dispela em i ~500 Congolese Francs long ron long sotpela hap.
reference: You can use boda-boda (motorcycle taxi) to get around Goma. The normal (local) price is ~500 Congolese Francs for the short ride.

source: Narapela samting tu em i olsem, "Timbuktu" em i hat long go long en na ol i save yusim dispela nem olsem tok piksa bilong ol samting i narapela kain, i stap long longwe ples.
reference: Combined with its relative inaccessibility, "Timbuktu" has come to be used as a metaphor for exotic, distant lands.

source: Tude, Timbuktu em i wanpela taun we i no gat planti samting, maski em i gat gutpela nem we i save pulim ol turis, na em i gat wanpela eapot.
reference: Today, Timbuktu is an impoverished town, although its reputation makes it a tourist attraction, and it has an airport.

source: Long 1990, ol i putim em i go long lista bilong 'world heritage sites' we i stap long mak nogut, bikos long bagarap i kamap long ol wesan bilong em long deset (desert).
reference: In 1990, it was added to the list of world heritage sites in danger, due to the threat of desert sands.

source: Em i bin wanpela ples we PBS progrem bilong Henry Louis Gates' Wonders of the African World i mekim wanpela bikpela stop na lukluk long en.
reference: It was one of the major stops during Henry Louis Gates' PBS special Wonders of the African World.

source: Dispela siti em i narapela kain long olgeta narapela siti bilong kantri, bikos em i luk moa olsem ples bilong Arab na i no olsem ples bilong Africa.
reference: The city is in stark contrast to the rest of the country's cities, because it has more of an Arabic flair than of an African.

source: Kruger National Park (KNP) i stap long hap 'north-east' bilong South Africa na i ran arere long bod bilong Mozambique long is, Zimbabwe long not, na boda long hap saut em Crocodile River.
reference: The Kruger National Park (KNP) lies in the north-east of South Africa and runs along the border of Mozambique in the east, Zimbabwe in the north, and the southern border is the Crocodile River.

source: Dispela park i karamapim 19,500 km² na em i bruk i go long 14-pela 'ecozone', wanwan bilong ol i sapotim narakain narakain 'wildlfie'.
reference: The park covers 19,500 km² and is divided in 14 different ecozones, each supporting different wildlife.

source: Em i wanpela bilong ol bikpela samting we i save pulim ol turis i go long South Africa na ol i save lukim em olsem impoten samting insait long South African National Parks (SANParks).
reference: It is one of the main attractions of South Africa and it is considered the flagship of South African National Parks (SANParks).

source: Wankain olsem olgeta South African National Parks, i gat 'conservation' na 'entry fee' bilong wanwan de long ol park.
reference: As with all South African National Parks, there are daily conservation and entry fees for the park.

source: Em i gutpela long wanpela long baim wanpela Wild Card, we i save givim tok orait long i go insait long ol park insait long South Africa o olgeta South African National Parks.
reference: It may also be beneficial for one to buy a Wild Card, which provides entry to either selections of parks in South Africa or all of the South African National Parks.

source: Hong Kong Island i givim teritori (territory) bilong Hong Kong nem bilong na em i ples we planti turis i save go long en.
reference: Hong Kong Island gives the territory of Hong Kong its name and is the place that many tourists regard as the main focus.
