source: Long Japan, empera (emperor) i save holim ol pati bilong ol namba wan 'cherry blossom' pati bilong em yet na ol memba bilong 'aristocracy' long Imperial Court.
reference: In Japan, the first cherry blossom parties were hosted by the emperor only for himself and other members of the aristocracy around the Imperial Court.

source: Ol plent (plant) i save luk nais tru insait long wel envaironmen bilong ol, olsem an no ken traim long rausim "wanpela tasol" na kisim olsem ‘specimen’.
reference: Plants look their best when in a natural environment, so resist the temptation to remove even "just one" specimen.

source: Sapos yu go long wanpela gaden we ol i stretim bilong ol lalin i kam na lukim, pasin bilong kolektim ol "'specimen’" bai mekim na ol i rausim yu, na ol i no inap toktok planti.
reference: If visiting a formally arranged garden, collecting "specimens" is also going to get you ejected, without discussion.

source: Singapore em i seif tru na isi long i go i kam, na yu ken baim kainkain samting taim yu kamap long hap.
reference: Singapore is generally an extremely safe place to be and very easy to navigate, and you can buy almost anything after arriving.

source: Tasol bikos em i stap long "‘high tropics’" sampela ‘degree’ not long ‘equator’ yu bai nid long stap insait long hot (olgeta taim) na strongpela san (taim san i klia, dispela i no save kamap planti taim).
reference: But being placed in the "high tropics" just a few degrees north of equator you will need to deal with both heat (always) and strong sun (when the sky is clear, more rarely).

source: Na tu, i gat sampela bas (bus) i save go long hap not long Hebron, dispela em i ples we ol i planim ol papa bilong bilip insait long Baibel Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, na ol meri bilong ol.
reference: There are also a few buses going north to Hebron, the traditional burial place of the Biblical patriarchs Abraham, Isaac, Jacob, and their wives.

source: Sekim sapos ol bas (bus) yu tingting long kisim em bai go insait long Hebron na i no go klostu long setelmen (settlement) bilong ol Jewish long Kiryat Arba.
reference: Check that the bus you are thinking of taking goes into Hebron and not just to the nearby Jewish settlement of Kiryat Arba.

source: Ol wara we i stap insait long ples em ol gutpela samting bilong mekim holide long en.
reference: Inland waterways can be a good theme to base a holiday around.

source: Eksampel, taim yu go lukim ol kasol (castle) insait long Loire Valley, Rhine valley o kisim wanpela ‘cruise’ i go long ol naispela siti long Danube o ‘boating’ long Erie Canal.
reference: For example visiting castles in the Loire Valley, the Rhine valley or taking a cruise to interesting cites on the Danube or boating along the Erie Canal.

source: Na tu, ol i save makim ol ‘route’ we planti lain i save go wokabaut o ‘cycling’ long em.
reference: They also define routes for popular hiking and cycling trails.

source: Krismas em i wanpela bilong ol impoten holide bilong Christianity, na ol i save amamasim dispela de olsem bonde bilong Jisas.
reference: Christmas is one of the most important holidays of Christianity, and is celebrated as the birthday of Jesus.

source: Planti bilong ol pasin we ol lain i save mekim long dispela holide em ol lain i no Kristen na ol kantri we i no Kristen kantri i save bihainim long olgeta hap bilong graun.
reference: Many of the traditions surrounding the holiday have been adopted also by non-believers in Christian countries and non-Christians around the world.

source: I gat tredisen (tradition) i bilong no ken slip long Easter nait na stap long wanpela ples klia long lukim san i kirap.
reference: There's a tradition to pass the Easter night awake at some exposed point to see the sunrise.

source: I gat ol Kristen bilip we i toktok long dispela tredisen (tradition), tasol em i wankain olsem tredisen bilong Spring na Fertility bipo long taim bilong Kristen bilip.
reference: There are of course Christian theological explanations for this tradition, but it may well be a pre-Christian Spring and Fertility ritual.

source: Ol sios we i save bihainim pasin bilong lotu long bipo i save holim wanpela Easter Vigil long Sarere nai long Easter wiken, i gat ol manmeri bilong lotu i save i kam bung na ol i save mekim selebresen (celebration) long biknait i pinis na moning i kamap long amamas long Krais i kirap long dai.
reference: More traditional churches often hold an Easter Vigil on Saturday night during the Easter weekend, with the congregations often breaking into celebration at the stroke of midnight to celebrate Christ's resurrection.

source: Olgeta animal we i kam kamap long ailen i kam long hia taim ol i swim, flai o trip antap long wara.
reference: All animals that originally arrived in the islands came here either by swimming, flying or floating.

source: Bikos long longpela distens (distance) long kontinen (continent) ol 'mammal' i no inap long mekim longpela wokabaut na dispela i mekim 'giant tortoise' i kamap olsem nambawan animal bilong kaikai gras insait long Galapagos.
reference: Due to the long distance from the continent mammals were unable to make the journey making the giant tortoise the primary grazing animal in the Galapagos.

source: Taim ol man i kam long Galapagos, planti 'mammal' we ol i bin kisim i kam em meme, hos, kau, rat, pusi na dok.
reference: Since the arrival of man to the Galapagos, many mammals have been introduced including goats, horses, cows, rats, cats and dogs.

source: Sapos yu go lukim Arctic o Antarctic eria long taim bilong winta (winter) yu bai lukim 'polar night', dispela em i min olsem san i no inap kirap long 'horizon'.
reference: If you visit the Arctic or Antarctic areas in the winter you will experience the polar night, which means that the sun doesn't rise above the horizon.

source: Dispela em i gutpela sans bilong lukim Aurora borealis, taim skai bai tudak long klostu olgeta aua long kilok.
reference: This offers a good opportunity to see the Aurora borealis, as the sky will be dark more or less around the clock.

source: Bikos ol dispela eria i no gat planti manmeri, planti taim 'light pollution' em bai i no wanpela problem, yu bai inap long lukim ol sta.
reference: As the areas are sparsely populated, and light pollution therefore often not a problem, you will also be able to enjoy the stars.

source: Japanese kalsa bilong wok em i gat oda bilong em na em i moa fomol (formal) long we ol Westerner i save long en.
reference: Japanese work culture is more hierarchical and formal that what Westerners may be used to.

source: Ol 'suit' em ol stendet bisnis klos, na ol wanwok i save kolim wanpela narapela long nem bilong famili bilong ol o ol taitol bilong ol long wok.
reference: Suits are standard business attire, and coworkers call each other by their family names or by job titles.

source: Gutpela sindaun insait long wok ples em i bikpela samting, na ol i laikim grup wok na i no pasin bilong tok amamas long samting we wanpela wokman i mekim.
reference: Workplace harmony is crucial, emphasizing group effort rather than praising individual accomplishments.

source: Ol woklain i mas kisim tok orait long ol lain i antap long ol bipo long ol i mekim wanpela disisen, na ol i mas harim tok stia bilong ol lain we ol i wok aninit long en na ol i no ken askim kwesten.
reference: Workers must often get their superiors' approval for any decisions they make, and are expected to obey their superiors' instructions without question.
